ConfigState.title: ConfigState

Container.description:
The Container object describes an instance of a specific container. A container
is a prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing
system using a container runtime like containerd.

hash.description: Commit hash of image created for docker or the SHA256 hash of the container. For example: 13550340a8681c84c861aac2e5b440161c2b33a3e4f302ac680ca5b686de48de.

image.description: The container image used as a template to run the container.

labels.description: The list of labels associated to the container.

name.description: The container name.

network_driver.description:
The network driver used by the container. For example, bridge, overlay, host,
none, etc.

orchestrator.description: The orchestrator managing the container, such as ECS, EKS, K8s, or OpenShift.

pod_uuid.description:
The unique identifier of the pod (or equivalent) that the container is
executing on.

runtime.description: The backend running the container, such as containerd or cri-o.

size.description: The size of the container image.

tag.description: The tag used by the container. It can indicate version, format, OS.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the container.

uid.description: The full container unique identifier for this instantiation of the container. For example: ac2ea168264a08f9aaca0dfc82ff3551418dfd22d02b713142a6843caa2f61bf.

Container.title: Container

CpuArchitectureIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the CPU architecture.
CpuArchitectureIdEnum.title: CpuArchitectureIdEnum

Cve.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) object represents publicly disclosed cybersecurity vulnerabilities defined in CVE Program catalog (CVE). There is one CVE Record for each vulnerability in the catalog.

created_time.description:
The Record Creation Date identifies when the CVE ID was issued to a CVE
Numbering Authority (CNA) or the CVE Record was published on the CVE List. Note
that the Record Creation Date does not necessarily indicate when this
vulnerability was discovered, shared with the affected vendor, publicly
disclosed, or updated in CVE.

cvss.description: The CVSS object details Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) scores from the advisory that are related to the vulnerability.

cwe.description: The CWE object represents a weakness in a software system that can be exploited by a threat actor to perform an attack. The CWE object is based on the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) catalog.

cwe_uid.description: The Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) unique identifier. For example: CWE-787.

cwe_url.description: Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) definition URL. For example: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html.

desc.description: A brief description of the CVE Record.

epss.description: The Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) object describes the estimated probability a vulnerability will be exploited. EPSS is a community-driven effort to combine descriptive information about vulnerabilities (CVEs) with evidence of actual exploitation in-the-wild. (EPSS).

modified_time.description: The Record Modified Date identifies when the CVE record was last updated.

product.description: The product where the vulnerability was discovered.

references.description: A list of reference URLs with additional information about the CVE Record.

related_cwes.description: Describes the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) details related to the CVE Record.

title.description: A title or a brief phrase summarizing the CVE record.

type.description:
The vulnerability type as selected from a large dropdown menu during CVE refinement.

Most frequently used vulnerability types are: DoS, Code Execution, Overflow, Memory Corruption, Sql Injection, XSS, Directory Traversal, Http Response Splitting, Bypass something, Gain Information, Gain Privileges, CSRF, File Inclusion. For more information see Vulnerabilities By Type distributions.

uid.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures unique number assigned to a specific computer vulnerability. A CVE Identifier begins with 4 digits representing the year followed by a sequence of digits that acts as a unique identifier. For example: CVE-2021-12345.

Cve.title: Cve

Cvss.description: The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) object provides a way to capture the principal characteristics of a vulnerability and produce a numerical score reflecting its severity.

base_score.description: The CVSS base score. For example: 9.1.

depth.description: The CVSS depth represents a depth of the equation used to calculate CVSS score.

metrics.description: The Common Vulnerability Scoring System metrics. This attribute contains information on the CVE's impact. If the CVE has been analyzed, this attribute will contain any CVSSv2 or CVSSv3 information associated with the vulnerability. For example: { {"Access Vector", "Network"}, {"Access Complexity", "Low"}, ...}.

overall_score.description: The CVSS overall score, impacted by base, temporal, and environmental metrics. For example: 9.1.

severity.description:
The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) Qualitative Severity Rating. A textual representation of the numeric score.

CVSS v2.0
  • Low (0.0 – 3.9)
  • Medium (4.0 – 6.9)
  • High (7.0 – 10.0)

CVSS v3.0
  • None (0.0)
  • Low (0.1 - 3.9)
  • Medium (4.0 - 6.9)
  • High (7.0 - 8.9)
  • Critical (9.0 - 10.0)

src_url.description: The source URL for the CVSS score. For example: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-44228

vector_string.description: The CVSS vector string is a text representation of a set of CVSS metrics. It is commonly used to record or transfer CVSS metric information in a concise form. For example: 3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H.

vendor_name.description: The vendor that provided the CVSS score. For example: NVD, REDHAT etc.

version.description: The CVSS version. For example: 3.1.

Cvss.title: Cvss

Cwe.description: The CWE object represents a weakness in a software system that can be exploited by a threat actor to perform an attack. The CWE object is based on the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) catalog.

caption.description: The caption assigned to the Common Weakness Enumeration unique identifier.

src_url.description: URL pointing to the CWE Specification. For more information see CWE.

uid.description: The Common Weakness Enumeration unique number assigned to a specific weakness. A CWE Identifier begins "CWE" followed by a sequence of digits that acts as a unique identifier. For example: CWE-123.

Cwe.title: Cwe

D3fTactic.description:
The MITRE D3FEND™ Tactic object describes the tactic ID and/or name that is
associated to an attack.

name.description: The tactic name that is associated with the defensive technique. For example: Isolate.

src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the defensive tactic. For example: https://d3fend.mitre.org/tactic/d3f:Isolate/.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the defensive tactic.

D3fTactic.title: D3fTactic

D3fTechnique.description:
The MITRE D3FEND™ Technique object describes the leaf defensive technique ID
and/or name associated to a countermeasure.

name.description: The name of the defensive technique. For example: IO Port Restriction.

src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the defensive technique. For example: https://d3fend.mitre.org/technique/d3f:IOPortRestriction/.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the defensive technique. For example: D3-IOPR.

D3fTechnique.title: D3fTechnique

D3fend.description:
The MITRE D3FEND™ object describes the tactic & technique associated with a
countermeasure.

d3f_tactic.description:
The Tactic object describes the tactic ID and/or name that is associated with a
countermeasure.

d3f_technique.description:
The Technique object describes the technique ID and/or name associated with a
countermeasure.

version.description: The D3FEND™ Matrix version.

D3fend.title: D3fend

DataClassification.description:
The Data Classification object includes information about data classification
levels and data category types.

category.description:
The name of the data classification category that data matched into, e.g.
Financial, Personal, Governmental, etc.

category_id.description: The normalized identifier of the data classification category.

classifier_details.description: Describes details about the classifier used for data classification.

confidentiality.description:
The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value.
In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.

discovery_details.description: Details about the data discovered by classification job.

policy.description:
Details about the data policy that governs data handling and security measures
related to classification.

src_url.description: The source URL pointing towards the full classification job details.

status.description: The resultant status of the classification job normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_details.description: The contextual description of the status, status_id value.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the classification job.

total.description: The total count of discovered entities, by the classification job.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the classification job.

DataClassification.title: DataClassification

DataClassificationCategoryIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the data classification category.
DataClassificationCategoryIdEnum.title: DataClassificationCategoryIdEnum

DataClassificationStatusIdEnum.description: The normalized status identifier of the classification job.
DataClassificationStatusIdEnum.title: DataClassificationStatusIdEnum

DataLifecycleStateIdEnum.description:
The stage or state that the data was in when it was assessed or scanned by a
data security tool.
DataLifecycleStateIdEnum.title: DataLifecycleStateIdEnum

DataSecurity.description:
The Data Security object describes the characteristics, techniques and content
of a Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Data Loss Detection (DLD), Data
Classification, or similar tools' finding, alert, or detection mechanism(s).

category.description:
The name of the data classification category that data matched into, e.g.
Financial, Personal, Governmental, etc.

category_id.description: The normalized identifier of the data classification category.

classifier_details.description: Describes details about the classifier used for data classification.

confidentiality.description:
The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value.
In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.

data_lifecycle_state.description:
The name of the stage or state that the data was in. E.g., Data-at-Rest,
Data-in-Transit, etc.

data_lifecycle_state_id.description:
The stage or state that the data was in when it was assessed or scanned by a
data security tool.

detection_pattern.description: Specific pattern, algorithm, fingerprint, or model used for detection.

detection_system.description:
The name of the type of data security tool or system that the finding,
detection, or alert originated from. E.g., Endpoint, Secure Email Gateway, etc.

detection_system_id.description:
The type of data security tool or system that the finding, detection, or alert
originated from.

discovery_details.description: Details about the data discovered by classification job.

pattern_match.description: A text, binary, file name, or datastore that matched against a detection rule.

policy.description: Details about the policy that triggered the finding.

src_url.description: The source URL pointing towards the full classification job details.

status.description: The resultant status of the classification job normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_details.description: The contextual description of the status, status_id value.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the classification job.

total.description: The total count of discovered entities, by the classification job.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the classification job.

DataSecurity.title: DataSecurity

DataSecurityFinding.description:
A Data Security Finding describes detections or alerts generated by various data security products such as Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Data Classification, Secrets Management, Digital Rights Management (DRM), Data Security Posture Management (DSPM), and similar tools. These detections or alerts can be created using fingerprinting, statistical analysis, machine learning or other methodologies. The finding describes the actors and endpoints who accessed or own the sensitive data, as well as the resources which store the sensitive data. Note: if the event producer is a security control, the security_control profile should be applied and its attacks information, if present, should be duplicated into the finding_info object.
Note: If the Finding is an incident, i.e. requires incident workflow, also apply the incident profile or aggregate this finding into an Incident Finding.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the Data Security Finding activity.

activity_name.description: The Data Security finding activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
Describes details about the actor implicated in the data security finding.
Either an actor that owns a particular digital file or information store, or an
actor which accessed classified or sensitive data.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

assignee.description: The details of the user assigned to an Incident.

assignee_group.description: The details of the group assigned to an Incident.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

comment.description: A user provided comment about the finding.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

data_security.description:
The Data Security object describes the characteristics, techniques and content
of a Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Data Loss Detection (DLD), Data
Classification, or similar tools' finding, alert, or detection mechanism(s).

database.description:
Describes the database where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was
accessed from. Databases are typically datastore services that contain an
organized collection of structured and/or semi-structured data.

databucket.description:
Describes the databucket where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or
was accessed from. The data bucket object is a basic container that holds data,
typically organized through the use of data partitions.

device.description:
Describes the device where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was
accessed from.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description:
Describes the endpoint where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was
accessed from.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event included in the finding.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

file.description: Describes a file that contains classified or sensitive data.

finding_info.description: Describes the supporting information about a generated finding.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

impact.description:
The impact , normalized to the caption of the impact_id value. In the case of
'Other', it is defined by the event source.

impact_id.description:
The normalized impact of the incident or finding. Per NIST, this is the
magnitude of harm that can be expected to result from the consequences of
unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction, or loss of information or
information system availability.

impact_score.description: The impact as an integer value of the finding, valid range 0-100.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. For example, an activity_id of 'Create' could constitute an alertable signal and the value would be true, while 'Close' likely would not and either omit the attribute or set its value to false. Note that other events with the security_control profile may also be deemed alertable signals and may also carry is_alert = true attributes.

is_suspected_breach.description: A determination based on analytics as to whether a potential breach was found.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

priority.description:
The priority, normalized to the caption of the priority_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

priority_id.description:
The normalized priority. Priority identifies the relative importance of the
incident or finding. It is a measurement of urgency.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

resources.description:
Describes details about additional resources, where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was accessed from.

You can populate this object, if the specific resource type objects available in the class (database, databucket, table, file) aren't sufficient; OR
You can also choose to duplicate uid, name of the specific resources objects, for a consistent access to resource uids across all findings.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description:
Details about the source endpoint where classified or sensitive data was
accessed from.

src_url.description: A Url link used to access the original incident.

start_time.description: The time of the least recent event included in the finding.

status.description:
The normalized status of the Finding set by the consumer normalized to the
caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the
source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the Finding, set by the consumer.

table.description:
Describes the table where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was
accessed from. The table object represents a table within a structured
relational database, warehouse, lake, or similar.

ticket.description: The linked ticket in the ticketing system.

tickets.description:
The associated ticket(s) in the ticketing system. Each ticket contains details
like ticket ID, status, etc.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.

verdict.description: The verdict assigned to an Incident finding.

verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict of an Incident.

DataSecurityFinding.title: DataSecurityFinding

DataSecurityFindingActivityIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the Data Security Finding activity.
DataSecurityFindingActivityIdEnum.title: DataSecurityFindingActivityIdEnum

Database.description:
The database object is used for databases which are typically datastore
services that contain an organized collection of structured and unstructured
data or a types of data.

created_time.description: The time when the database was known to have been created.

data_classification.description:
The Data Classification object includes information about data classification
levels and data category types.

data_classifications.description:
A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data
classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

desc.description: The description of the database.

embedding_model.description: Model used for creating embeddings (if applicable). For example: text-embedding-ada-002 or all-MiniLM-L6-v2.

groups.description: The group names to which the database belongs.

modified_time.description:
The most recent time when any changes, updates, or modifications were made
within the database.

name.description: The database name, ordinarily as assigned by a database administrator.

size.description: The size of the database in bytes.

type.description: The database type.

type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the database type.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the database.

Database.title: Database

DatabaseTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the database type.
DatabaseTypeIdEnum.title: DatabaseTypeIdEnum

Databucket.description:
The databucket object is a basic container that holds data, typically organized
through the use of data partitions.

agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

cloud_partition.description:
The logical grouping or isolated segment within a cloud provider's
infrastructure where the databucket is located.

created_time.description: The time when the databucket was known to have been created.

criticality.description: The criticality of the databucket as defined by the event source.

data_classification.description:
The Data Classification object includes information about data classification
levels and data category types.

data_classifications.description:
A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data
classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

desc.description: The description of the databucket.

encryption_details.description:
The encryption details of the databucket. Should be populated if the databucket
is encrypted.

file.description: Details about the file/object within a databucket.

group.description: The name of the related resource group.

groups.description: The group names to which the databucket belongs.

hostname.description: The fully qualified hostname of the databucket.

ip.description: The IP address of the resource, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

is_backed_up.description: Indicates whether the device or resource has a backup enabled, such as an automated snapshot or a cloud backup. For example, this is indicated by the cloudBackupEnabled value within JAMF Pro mobile devices or the registration of an AWS ARN with the AWS Backup service.

is_encrypted.description: Indicates if the databucket is encrypted.

is_public.description: Indicates if the databucket is publicly accessible.

labels.description: The list of labels associated to the resource.

modified_time.description:
The most recent time when any changes, updates, or modifications were made
within the databucket.

name.description: The databucket name.

namespace.description:
The namespace is useful when similar entities exist that you need to keep
separate.

owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the databucket.

region.description: The cloud region of the databucket.

resource_relationship.description:
A graph representation showing how this databucket relates to and interacts
with other entities in the environment. This can include parent/child
relationships, dependencies, or other connections.

size.description: The size of the databucket in bytes.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the resource.

type.description: The databucket type.

type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the databucket type.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the databucket.

uid_alt.description: The alternative unique identifier of the resource.

version.description: The version of the resource. For example 1.2.3.

zone.description:
The specific availability zone within a cloud region where the databucket is
located.

Databucket.title: Databucket

DatabucketTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the databucket type.
DatabucketTypeIdEnum.title: DatabucketTypeIdEnum

DatastoreActivity.description:
Datastore events describe general activities (Read, Update, Query, Delete,
etc.) which affect datastores or data within those datastores, e.g. (AWS RDS,
AWS S3).

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

ai_model.description:
The AI Model object describes the characteristics of an AI/ML model. Examples
include language models like GPT-4, embedding models like
text-embedding-ada-002, and computer vision models like CLIP.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

database.description:
The database object is used for databases which are typically datastore
services that contain an organized collection of structured and unstructured
data or a types of data.

databucket.description:
The data bucket object is a basic container that holds data, typically
organized through the use of data partitions.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description: Details about the endpoint hosting the datastore application or service.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

http_request.description: Details about the underlying http request.

http_response.description: Details about the underlying http response.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

message_context.description:
Communication context for AI system interactions including protocols, roles,
clients, and session information for MCP and other AI communication systems.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

query_info.description:
The query info object holds information related to data access within a
datastore. To access, manipulate, delete, or retrieve data from a datastore, a
database query must be written using a specific syntax.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description: Details about the source of the activity.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

table.description:
The table object represents a table within a structured relational database or
datastore, which contains columns and rows of data that are able to be create,
updated, deleted and queried.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type.description: The datastore resource type (e.g. database, datastore, or table).

type_id.description: The normalized datastore resource type identifier.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

DatastoreActivity.title: DatastoreActivity

DatastoreActivityTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized datastore resource type identifier.
DatastoreActivityTypeIdEnum.title: DatastoreActivityTypeIdEnum

DceRpc.description:
The DCE/RPC, or Distributed Computing Environment/Remote Procedure Call, object
describes the remote procedure call system for distributed computing
environments.

command_response.description: The reply to the request command (e.g. RESPONSE, BINDACK or FAULT).

opnum.description:
An operation number used to identify a specific remote procedure call (RPC)
method or a method in an interface.

rpc_interface.description:
The RPC Interface object describes the details pertaining to the remote
procedure call interface.

DceRpc.title: DceRpc

DepthEnum.description: The CVSS depth represents a depth of the equation used to calculate CVSS score.
DepthEnum.title: DepthEnum

DetectionFinding.description:
A Detection Finding describes detections or alerts generated by security products using correlation engines, detection engines or other methodologies. Note: if the event producer is a security control, the security_control profile should be applied and its attacks information, if present, should be duplicated into the finding_info object.
Note: If the Finding is an incident, i.e. requires incident workflow, also apply the incident profile or aggregate this finding into an Incident Finding.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the finding activity.

activity_name.description: The finding activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

anomaly_analyses.description:
Describes baseline information about normal activity patterns, along with any
detected deviations or anomalies that triggered this finding.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

assignee.description: The details of the user assigned to an Incident.

assignee_group.description: The details of the group assigned to an Incident.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

comment.description: A user provided comment about the finding.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description:
Describes the affected device/host. If applicable, it can be used in conjunction with Resource(s).

e.g. Specific details about an AWS EC2 instance, that is affected by the Finding.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event included in the finding.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

evidences.description:
Describes various evidence artifacts associated to the activity/activities that
triggered a security detection.

finding_info.description: Describes the supporting information about a generated finding.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

impact.description:
The impact , normalized to the caption of the impact_id value. In the case of
'Other', it is defined by the event source.

impact_id.description:
The normalized impact of the incident or finding. Per NIST, this is the
magnitude of harm that can be expected to result from the consequences of
unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction, or loss of information or
information system availability.

impact_score.description: The impact as an integer value of the finding, valid range 0-100.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. For example, an activity_id of 'Create' could constitute an alertable signal and the value would be true, while 'Close' likely would not and either omit the attribute or set its value to false. Note that other events with the security_control profile may also be deemed alertable signals and may also carry is_alert = true attributes.

is_suspected_breach.description: A determination based on analytics as to whether a potential breach was found.

malware.description: Describes malware reported in a Detection Finding.

malware_scan_info.description: Describes details about malware scan job that triggered this Detection Finding.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

priority.description:
The priority, normalized to the caption of the priority_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

priority_id.description:
The normalized priority. Priority identifies the relative importance of the
incident or finding. It is a measurement of urgency.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

remediation.description: Describes the recommended remediation steps to address identified issue(s).

resources.description:
Describes details about resources that were the target of the activity that
triggered the finding.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_url.description: A Url link used to access the original incident.

start_time.description: The time of the least recent event included in the finding.

status.description:
The normalized status of the Finding set by the consumer normalized to the
caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the
source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the Finding, set by the consumer.

ticket.description: The linked ticket in the ticketing system.

tickets.description:
The associated ticket(s) in the ticketing system. Each ticket contains details
like ticket ID, status, etc.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.

verdict.description: The verdict assigned to an Incident finding.

verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict of an Incident.

vulnerabilities.description: Describes vulnerabilities reported in a Detection Finding.

DetectionFinding.title: DetectionFinding

DetectionPatternTypeIdEnum.description:
Specifies the type of detection pattern used to identify the associated threat
indicator.
DetectionPatternTypeIdEnum.title: DetectionPatternTypeIdEnum

DetectionSystemIdEnum.description:
The type of data security tool or system that the finding, detection, or alert
originated from.
DetectionSystemIdEnum.title: DetectionSystemIdEnum

Device.description:
The Device object represents an addressable computer system or host, which is
typically connected to a computer network and participates in the transmission
or processing of data within the computer network.

agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

autoscale_uid.description: The unique identifier of the cloud autoscale configuration.

boot_time.description: The time the system was booted.

boot_uid.description: A unique identifier of the device that changes after every reboot. For example, the value of /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id from Linux's procfs.

container.description:
The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a
prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system
using a container runtime like containerd.

created_time.description: The time when the device was known to have been created.

desc.description: The description of the device, ordinarily as reported by the operating system.

domain.description: The network domain where the device resides. For example: work.example.com.

eid.description:
An Embedded Identity Document, is a unique serial number that identifies an
eSIM-enabled device.

first_seen_time.description: The initial discovery time of the device.

groups.description: The group names to which the device belongs. For example: ["Windows Laptops", "Engineering"].

hypervisor.description: The name of the hypervisor running on the device. For example, Xen, VMware, Hyper-V, VirtualBox, etc.

iccid.description:
The Integrated Circuit Card Identification of a mobile device. Typically it is
a unique 18 to 22 digit number that identifies a SIM card.

image.description: The image used as a template to run the virtual machine.

imei.description: The International Mobile Equipment Identity that is associated with the device.

imei_list.description:
The International Mobile Equipment Identity values that are associated with the
device.

instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.

interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).

interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.

ip.description: The device IP address, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

is_backed_up.description: Indicates whether the device or resource has a backup enabled, such as an automated snapshot or a cloud backup. For example, this is indicated by the cloudBackupEnabled value within JAMF Pro mobile devices or the registration of an AWS ARN with the AWS Backup service.

is_compliant.description: The event occurred on a compliant device.

is_managed.description: The event occurred on a managed device.

is_mobile_account_active.description: Indicates whether the device has an active mobile account. For example, this is indicated by the itunesStoreAccountActive value within JAMF Pro mobile devices.

is_personal.description: The event occurred on a personal device.

is_shared.description: The event occurred on a shared device.

is_supervised.description:
The event occurred on a supervised device. Devices that are supervised are
typically mobile devices managed by a Mobile Device Management solution and are
restricted from specific behaviors such as Apple AirDrop.

is_trusted.description: The event occurred on a trusted device.

last_seen_time.description: The most recent discovery time of the device.

location.description: The geographical location of the device.

mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.

mac_vendor.description:
The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller
(NIC), as identified from the MAC address.

meid.description:
The Mobile Equipment Identifier. It's a unique number that identifies a Code
Division Multiple Access (CDMA) mobile device.

model.description: The model of the device. For example ThinkPad X1 Carbon.

modified_time.description: The time when the device was last known to have been modified.

name.description:
The alternate device name, ordinarily as assigned by an administrator.

Note: The Name could be any other string that helps to identify the device, such as a phone number; for example 310-555-1234.

namespace_pid.description:
If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process
identifier within that process namespace.

network_interfaces.description:
The physical or virtual network interfaces that are associated with the device, one for each unique MAC address/IP address/hostname/name combination.

Note: The first element of the array is the network information that pertains to the event.

org.description: Organization and org unit related to the device.

os_machine_uuid.description: The operating system assigned Machine ID. In Windows, this is the value stored at the registry path: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid. In Linux, this is stored in the file: /etc/machine-id.

owner.description:
The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last
logged into it.

pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.

region.description: The region where the virtual machine is located. For example, an AWS Region.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.

type.description: The device type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.

type_id.description: The device type ID.

udid.description: The Apple assigned Unique Device Identifier (UDID). For iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, watchOS and visionOS devices, this is the UDID. For macOS devices, it is the Provisioning UDID. For example: 00008020-008D4548007B4F26

uid.description:
The unique identifier of the device. For example the Windows TargetSID or AWS
EC2 ARN.

uid_alt.description:
An alternate unique identifier of the device if any. For example the
ActiveDirectory DN.

vendor_name.description: The vendor for the device. For example Dell or Lenovo.

vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.

Device.title: Device

DeviceConfigStateChange.description:
Device Config State Change events report state changes that impact the security
of the device.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: The device that is impacted by the state change.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

prev_security_level.description: The previous security level of the entity

prev_security_level_id.description: The previous security level of the entity

prev_security_states.description: The previous security states of the device.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

security_level.description: The current security level of the entity

security_level_id.description: The current security level of the entity

security_states.description: The current security states of the device.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

state.description:
The Config Change Stat, normalized to the caption of the state_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

state_id.description: The Config Change State of the managed entity.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

DeviceConfigStateChange.title: DeviceConfigStateChange

DeviceConfigStateChangeStateIdEnum.description: The Config Change State of the managed entity.
