DeviceHwInfo.description:
The Device Hardware Information object contains details and specifications of
the physical components that make up a device. This information provides an
overview of the hardware capabilities, configuration, and characteristics of
the device.

bios_ver.description: The BIOS version. For example: LENOVO G5ETA2WW (2.62).

chassis.description: The chassis type describes the system enclosure or physical form factor. Such as the following examples for Windows Windows Chassis Types

cpu_architecture.description: The CPU architecture, normalized to the caption of the cpu_architecture_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the source.

cpu_architecture_id.description: The normalized identifier of the CPU architecture.

cpu_bits.description: The cpu architecture, the number of bits used for addressing in memory. For example: 32 or 64.

cpu_cores.description: The number of processor cores in all installed processors. For Example: 42.

cpu_count.description: The number of physical processors on a system. For example: 1.

cpu_speed.description: The speed of the processor in Mhz. For Example: 4200.

cpu_type.description: The processor type. For example: x86 Family 6 Model 37 Stepping 5.

desktop_display.description: The desktop display affiliated with the event

gpu_count.description: The number of GPU's on a system. For example: 1.

gpu_info_list.description:
A list of GPU objects describing the hardware properties of each graphics
processor installed on the device.

ram_size.description: The total amount of installed RAM, in Megabytes. For example: 2048.

uuid.description:
The device manufacturer assigned universally unique hardware identifier. For
SMBIOS compatible devices such as those running Linux and Windows, it is the
UUID member of the System Information structure in the SMBIOS information. For
macOS devices, it is the Hardware UUID (also known as IOPlatformUUID in the I/O
Registry).

DeviceHwInfo.title: DeviceHwInfo

DhcpActivity.description:
DHCP Activity events report MAC to IP assignment via DHCP from a client or
server.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

app_name.description:
The network application name identified by tools such as NBAR or App ID (e.g.,
youtube, facebook, webex). This represents a specific network application that
uses standard protocols (such as https or quic) to deliver its service.

app_protocol_name.description:
The application-layer (Layer 7) protocol name identified by deep packet inspection or packet parsing (e.g., https, quic, ssh, dns), expressed as an IANA-registered service name from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number Registry.

Note: Port numbers alone are not always a reliable indicator of the actual application protocol in use.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

cumulative_traffic.description: The cumulative (running total) network traffic aggregated from the start of a flow or session. Use when reporting: (1) total accumulated bytes/packets since flow initiation, (2) combined aggregation models where both incremental deltas and running totals are reported together (populate both traffic for the delta and this attribute for the cumulative total), or (3) final summary metrics when a long-lived connection closes. This represents the sum of all activity from flow start to the current observation, not a delta or point-in-time value.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description: The responder (server) of the DHCP connection.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

is_renewal.description: Indicates whether this is a lease/session renewal event.

ja4_fingerprint_list.description: A list of the JA4+ network fingerprints.

lease_dur.description:
This represents the length of the DHCP lease in seconds. This is present in
DHCP Ack events.

load_balancer.description:
The Load Balancer object contains information related to the device that is
distributing incoming traffic to specified destinations.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

network_observation_point.description: The network endpoint that observes or inspects network traffic as a third-party system, used when the observer is neither the source nor the destination of the communication (when observation_point_id = 3). Examples include network taps, span ports, inline security devices, or packet capture systems that monitor traffic between other endpoints.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

observation_point.description: Indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity. The value is normalized to the caption of the observation_point_id.

observation_point_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the observation point. The observation point
identifier indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network
endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

packet_list.description: The list of packet objects describing captured network packets.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_connection_info.description: The connection information from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_endpoint.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_http_request.description: The HTTP Request from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_http_response.description: The HTTP Response from the remote server to the proxy server.

proxy_tls.description: The TLS protocol negotiated between the proxy server and the remote server.

proxy_traffic.description:
The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from
proxy to remote server at a given point of time.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

relay.description: The network relay that is associated with the event.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description: The initiator (client) of the DHCP connection.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.

transaction_uid.description:
The unique identifier of the transaction. This is typically a random number
generated from the client to associate a dhcp request/response pair.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

DhcpActivity.title: DhcpActivity

DigitalSignature.description:
The Digital Signature object contains information about the cryptographic
mechanism used to verify the authenticity, integrity, and origin of the file or
application.

algorithm.description:
The digital signature algorithm used to create the signature, normalized to the
caption of 'algorithm_id'. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event
source.

algorithm_id.description: The identifier of the normalized digital signature algorithm.

certificate.description: The certificate object containing information about the digital certificate.

created_time.description: The time when the digital signature was created.

developer_uid.description: The developer ID on the certificate that signed the file.

digest.description:
The message digest attribute contains the fixed length message hash
representation and the corresponding hashing algorithm information.

state.description:
The digital signature state defines the signature state, normalized to the
caption of 'state_id'. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event
source.

state_id.description: The normalized identifier of the signature state.

DigitalSignature.title: DigitalSignature

DigitalSignatureAlgorithmIdEnum.description: The identifier of the normalized digital signature algorithm.
DigitalSignatureAlgorithmIdEnum.title: DigitalSignatureAlgorithmIdEnum

DigitalSignatureStateIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the signature state.
DigitalSignatureStateIdEnum.title: DigitalSignatureStateIdEnum

DirectionIdEnum.description:
The normalized identifier of the direction of the initiated connection,
traffic, or email.
DirectionIdEnum.title: DirectionIdEnum

DiscoveryDetails.description: The Discovery Details object describes results of a discovery task/job.

count.description: The number of discovered entities of the specified type.

occurrence_details.description:
Details about where in the target entity, specified information was discovered.
Only the attributes, relevant to the target entity type should be populated.

occurrences.description:
Details about where in the target entity, specified information was discovered.
Only the attributes, relevant to the target entity type should be populated.

type.description: The specific type of information that was discovered. e.g. name, phone_number, etc.

value.description: Optionally, the specific value of discovered information.

DiscoveryDetails.title: DiscoveryDetails

DiscoveryEvent.description:
The Discovery event is a generic event that defines a set of attributes
available in Discovery category events. As a generic event, it could be used to
log events that are not otherwise defined by the Discovery specific event
classes.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

Display.description:
The Display object contains information about the physical or virtual display
connected to a computer system.

physical_height.description: The numeric physical height of display.

physical_orientation.description: The numeric physical orientation of display.

physical_width.description: The numeric physical width of display.

scale_factor.description: The numeric scale factor of display.

Display.title: Display

DispositionIdEnum.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.
DispositionIdEnum.title: DispositionIdEnum

Dns.description:
The Domain Name System (DNS) object represents the shared information
associated with the DNS query and answer objects.

class_.description: The class of resource records being queried. See RFC1035. For example: IN.

packet_uid.description:
The DNS packet identifier assigned by the program that generated the query. The
identifier is copied to the response.

type.description: The type of resource records being queried. See RFC1035. For example: A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, and NS.

Dns.title: Dns

DnsActivity.description: DNS Activity events report DNS queries and answers as seen on the network.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

app_name.description:
The network application name identified by tools such as NBAR or App ID (e.g.,
youtube, facebook, webex). This represents a specific network application that
uses standard protocols (such as https or quic) to deliver its service.

app_protocol_name.description:
The application-layer (Layer 7) protocol name identified by deep packet inspection or packet parsing (e.g., https, quic, ssh, dns), expressed as an IANA-registered service name from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number Registry.

Note: Port numbers alone are not always a reliable indicator of the actual application protocol in use.

attacks.description:

sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

associated policies related to the activity/event.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

cumulative_traffic.description: The cumulative (running total) network traffic aggregated from the start of a flow or session. Use when reporting: (1) total accumulated bytes/packets since flow initiation, (2) combined aggregation models where both incremental deltas and running totals are reported together (populate both traffic for the delta and this attribute for the cumulative total), or (3) final summary metrics when a long-lived connection closes. This represents the sum of all activity from flow start to the current observation, not a delta or point-in-time value.

of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

DnsActivity.title: DnsActivity

DnsActivityRcodeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the DNS server response code. See RFC-6895.
DnsActivityRcodeIdEnum.title: DnsActivityRcodeIdEnum

DnsAnswer.description:
The DNS Answer object represents a specific response provided by the Domain
Name System (DNS) when querying for information about a domain or performing a
DNS operation. It encapsulates the relevant details and data returned by the
DNS server in response to a query.

class_.description: The class of DNS data contained in this resource record. See RFC1035. For example: IN.

flag_ids.description: The list of DNS answer header flag IDs.

flags.description: The list of DNS answer header flags.

packet_uid.description:
The DNS packet identifier assigned by the program that generated the query. The
identifier is copied to the response.

rdata.description:
The data describing the DNS resource. The meaning of this data depends on the
type and class of the resource record.

ttl.description:
The time interval that the resource record may be cached. Zero value means that
the resource record can only be used for the transaction in progress, and
should not be cached.

type.description: The type of data contained in this resource record. See RFC1035. For example: CNAME.

DnsAnswer.title: DnsAnswer

DnsAnswerFlagIdsEnum.description: The list of DNS answer header flag IDs.
DnsAnswerFlagIdsEnum.title: DnsAnswerFlagIdsEnum

DnsQuery.description:
The DNS query object represents a specific request made to the Domain Name
System (DNS) to retrieve information about a domain or perform a DNS operation.
This object encapsulates the necessary attributes and methods to construct and
send DNS queries, specify the query type (e.g., A, AAAA, MX).

class_.description: The class of resource records being queried. See RFC1035. For example: IN.

hostname.description: The hostname or domain being queried. For example: www.example.com

opcode.description: The DNS opcode specifies the type of the query message.

opcode_id.description: The DNS opcode ID specifies the normalized query message type as defined in RFC-5395.

packet_uid.description:
The DNS packet identifier assigned by the program that generated the query. The
identifier is copied to the response.

type.description: The type of resource records being queried. See RFC1035. For example: A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, and NS.

DnsQuery.title: DnsQuery

DnssecStatusIdEnum.description:
Describes the normalized status of DNS Security Extensions (DNSSEC) for a
domain.
DnssecStatusIdEnum.title: DnssecStatusIdEnum

DomainContact.description:
The contact information related to a domain registration, e.g., registrant,
administrator, abuse, billing, or technical contact.

location.description:
Location details for the contract such as the city, state/province, country,
etc.

name.description: The individual or organization name for the contact.

phone_number.description: The number associated with the phone.

type.description: The Domain Contact type, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source

type_id.description: The normalized domain contact type ID.

uid.description:
The unique identifier of the contact information, typically provided in WHOIS
information.

DomainContact.title: DomainContact

DomainContactTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized domain contact type ID.
DomainContactTypeIdEnum.title: DomainContactTypeIdEnum

DriveTypeIdEnum.description: Identifies the type of a disk drive, i.e. fixed, removable, etc.
DriveTypeIdEnum.title: DriveTypeIdEnum

DroneFlightsActivity.description: Drone Flights Activity events report the activity of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), their Operators, and mission-planning and authorization metadata as reported by the UAS platforms themselves, by Counter-UAS (CUAS) systems, or other remote monitoring or sensing infrastructure. Based on the Remote ID defined in Standard Specification for Remote ID and Tracking (ASTM Designation: F3411-22a) ASTM F3411-22a

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

auth_protocol.description: The authentication type as defined by the caption of auth_protocol_id. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

auth_protocol_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the authentication type used to authorize a flight
plan or mission.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

classification.description:
UA Classification - Allows a region to classify UAS in a regional specific
manner. The format may differ from region to region.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

comment.description:
This optional, free-text field enables the operator to describe the purpose of
a flight, if so desired.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description:
The destination network endpoint of the Unmanned Aerial System (UAS), Counter
Unmanned Aerial System (CUAS) platform, or other unmanned systems monitoring
and/or sensing infrastructure.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

protocol_name.description: The networking protocol associated with the Remote ID device or beacon. E.g. BLE, LTE, 802.11.

proxy_endpoint.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description:
The source network endpoint of the Unmanned Aerial System (UAS), Counter
Unmanned Aerial System (CUAS) platform, or other unmanned systems monitoring
and/or sensing infrastructure.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description: The normalized Operational status for the Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description:
The normalized Operational status identifier for the Unmanned Aerial System
(UAS).

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

traffic.description: Traffic refers to the amount of data transmitted from a Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) or Counter Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) (CUAS) system at a given point of time. Ex: bytes_in and bytes_out.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmanned_aerial_system.description: The Unmanned Aerial System object describes the characteristics, Position Location Information (PLI), and other metadata of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) and other unmanned and drone systems used in Remote ID. Remote ID is defined in the Standard Specification for Remote ID and Tracking (ASTM Designation: F3411-22a) ASTM F3411-22a.

unmanned_system_operating_area.description:
The UAS Operating Area object describes details about a precise area of
operations for a UAS flight or mission.

unmanned_system_operator.description: The human or machine operator of an Unmanned System.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

DroneFlightsActivity.title: DroneFlightsActivity

DroneFlightsActivityAuthProtocolIdEnum.description:
The normalized identifier of the authentication type used to authorize a flight
plan or mission.

DroneFlightsActivityStatusIdEnum.description:
The normalized Operational status identifier for the Unmanned Aerial System

Edge.description: Represents a connection or relationship between two nodes in a graph.

data.description: Additional data about the edge such as weight, distance, or custom properties.

is_directed.description: Indicates whether the edge is (true) or undirected (false).

name.description: The human-readable name or label for the edge.

relation.description: The type of relationship between nodes (e.g. is-attached-to , depends-on, etc).

source.description: The unique identifier of the node where the edge originates.

target.description: The unique identifier of the node where the edge terminates.

uid.description: Unique identifier of the edge.

Edge.title: Edge

Email.description:
The Email object describes the email metadata such as sender, recipients, and
direction, and can include embedded URLs and files.

cc.description: The machine-readable email header Cc values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.

cc_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header Cc Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

data_classification.description:
The Data Classification object includes information about data classification
levels and data category types.

data_classifications.description:
A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data
classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

delivered_to.description: The machine-readable Delivered-To email header field. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

delivered_to_list.description: The machine-readable Delivered-To email header values. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

files.description: The files embedded or attached to the email.

from_.description: The machine-readable email header From value, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.

from_list.description: The machine-readable email header From values. This array should contain the value in from. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.

from_mailbox.description: The human-readable email header From Mailbox value. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

from_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header From Mailbox values. This array should contain the value in from_mailbox. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

http_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers of an HTTP request or response.

is_read.description: The indication of whether the email has been read.

message_uid.description: The email header Message-ID value, as defined by RFC 5322.

reply_to.description: The machine-readable email header Reply-To value, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

reply_to_list.description: The machine-readable email header Reply-To values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

reply_to_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header Reply To Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

return_path.description: The address found in the 'Return-Path' header, which indicates where bounce messages (non-delivery reports) should be sent. This address is often set by the sending system and may differ from the 'From' or 'Sender' addresses. For example, mailer-daemon@senderserver.com.

sender.description: The machine readable email address of the system or server that actually transmitted the email message, extracted from the email headers per RFC 5322. This differs from the from field, which shows the message author. The sender field is most commonly used when multiple addresses appear in the from_list field, or when the transmitting system is different from the message author (such as when sending on behalf of someone else).

sender_mailbox.description: The human readable email address of the system or server that actually transmitted the email message, extracted from the email headers per RFC 5322. This differs from the from_mailbox field, which shows the message author. The sender mailbox field is most commonly used when multiple addresses appear in the from_mailboxes field, or when the transmitting system is different from the message author (such as when sending on behalf of someone else).

size.description: The size in bytes of the email, including attachments.

smtp_from.description: The value of the SMTP MAIL FROM command.

smtp_to.description: The value of the SMTP envelope RCPT TO command.

subject.description: The email header Subject value, as defined by RFC 5322.

to.description: The machine-readable email header To values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

to_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header To Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the email thread.

urls.description: The URLs embedded in the email.

x_originating_ip.description: The X-Originating-IP header identifying the emails originating IP address(es).

Email.title: Email

EmailActivity.description: Email Activity events report SMTP protocol and email activities including those with embedded URLs and files. See the Email object for details.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:

source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

attempt.description: The attempt number for attempting to deliver the email.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

banner.description:
The initial connection response that a messaging server receives after it
connects to an email server.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

command.description: The command issued by the initiator (client), such as SMTP HELO or EHLO.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

direction.description: The direction of the email, as defined by the direction_id value.

direction_id.description:
The direction of the email relative to the scanning host or organization.

Email scanned at an internet gateway might be characterized as inbound to the organization from the Internet, outbound from the organization to the Internet, or internal within the organization. Email scanned at a workstation might be characterized as inbound to, or outbound from the workstation.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description: The responder (server) receiving the email.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

email_auth.description: The SPF, DKIM and DMARC attributes of an email.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

from_.description:
The sender address from the transmission envelope. This reflects the actual
sending party and may differ from the 'From' header in the message.

Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

message_trace_uid.description:
The identifier that tracks a message that travels through multiple points of a

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

smtp_hello.description: The value of the SMTP HELO or EHLO command sent by the initiator (client).

src_endpoint.description: The initiator (client) sending the email.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

to.description:

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

unmapped.description:

Endpoint.description:
The Endpoint object describes a physical or virtual device that connects to and
exchanges information with a computer network. Some examples of endpoints are
mobile devices, desktop computers, virtual machines, embedded devices, and
servers. Internet-of-Things devices—like cameras, lighting, refrigerators,
security systems, smart speakers, and thermostats—are also endpoints.

agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

container.description:
The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a
prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system
using a container runtime like containerd.

domain.description:
The name of the domain that the endpoint belongs to or that corresponds to the
endpoint.

hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the endpoint.

instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.

interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).

interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.

ip.description: The IP address of the endpoint, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

location.description: The geographical location of the endpoint.

mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.

mac_vendor.description:
The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller
(NIC), as identified from the MAC address.

name.description: The short name of the endpoint.

namespace_pid.description:
If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process
identifier within that process namespace.

owner.description:
The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last
logged into it.

pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.

subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.

type.description: The endpoint type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.

type_id.description: The endpoint type ID.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint.

vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.

Endpoint.title: Endpoint

EndpointConnection.description:
The Endpoint Connection object contains information detailing a connection
attempt to an endpoint.

code.description: A numerical response status code providing details about the connection.

network_endpoint.description: Provides characteristics of the network endpoint.

EndpointConnection.title: EndpointConnection

Enrichment.description:
The Enrichment object provides inline enrichment data for specific attributes
of interest within an event. It serves as a mechanism to enhance or supplement
the information associated with the event by adding additional relevant details
or context.

created_time.description: The time when the enrichment data was generated.

data.description:
The enrichment data associated with the attribute and value. The meaning of
this data depends on the type the enrichment record.

desc.description: A long description of the enrichment data.

name.description: The name of the attribute to which the enriched data pertains.

reputation.description: The reputation of the enrichment data.

short_desc.description: A short description of the enrichment data.

src_url.description: The URL of the source of the enrichment data.

type.description: The enrichment type. For example: location.

value.description: The value of the attribute to which the enriched data pertains.

Enrichment.title: Enrichment

Entity.description:
The Entity object is an unordered collection of attributes, with a name and
unique identifier. It serves as a base object that defines a set of attributes
and default constraints available in all objects that extend it.

name.description: The name of the entity.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the entity.

Entity.title: Entity

or a user at a management console. The activity can be a create, read, update,

Epss.description: The Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) object describes the estimated probability a vulnerability will be exploited. EPSS is a community-driven effort to combine descriptive information about vulnerabilities (CVEs) with evidence of actual exploitation in-the-wild. (EPSS).

created_time.description: The timestamp indicating when the EPSS score was calculated.

percentile.description:
The EPSS score's percentile representing relative importance and ranking of the
score in the larger EPSS dataset.

score.description:
The EPSS score representing the probability [0-1] of exploitation in the wild
in the next 30 days (following score publication).

version.description: The version of the EPSS model used to calculate the score.

EvidenceInfo.description:
Data collected directly from devices that represents forensic information
pulled, queried, or discovered from devices that may indicate malicious
activity. It contains a number of child objects, each representing a distinct
evidence domain (network connections, file artifacts, registry entries, etc.).
When mapping raw telemetry data users should select Query Evidence and then the
appropriate child object that best matches the evidence type.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description:
An addressable device, computer system or host from which evidence was
collected.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

query_evidence.description:
The specific resulting evidence information that was queried or discovered
based on the query type. Contains various child objects corresponding to the
query_type_id values.

query_info.description: The search details associated with the query request.

query_result_id.description: The normalized identifier of the query result.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

EvidenceInfo.title: EvidenceInfo

Evidences.description:
A collection of evidence artifacts associated to the activity/activities that
triggered a security detection.

actor.description:
Describes details about the user/role/process that was the source of the
activity that triggered the detection.

api.description:
Describes details about the API call associated to the activity that triggered
the detection.

connection_info.description:
Describes details about the network connection associated to the activity that
triggered the detection.

container.description:
Describes details about the container associated to the activity that triggered
the detection.

data.description: Additional evidence data that is not accounted for in the specific evidence attributes. Use only when absolutely necessary.

database.description:
Describes details about the database associated to the activity that triggered
the detection.

databucket.description:
Describes details about the databucket associated to the activity that
triggered the detection.

device.description:
An addressable device, computer system or host associated to the activity that
triggered the detection.

dst_endpoint.description:
Describes details about the destination of the network activity that triggered
the detection.

email.description: The email object associated to the activity that triggered the detection.

file.description:
Describes details about the file associated to the activity that triggered the
detection.

http_request.description:
Describes details about the http request associated to the activity that
triggered the detection.

http_response.description:
Describes details about the http response associated to the activity that
triggered the detection.

ja4_fingerprint_list.description: Describes details about the JA4+ fingerprints that triggered the detection.

job.description:
Describes details about the scheduled job that was associated with the activity
that triggered the detection.

name.description: The naming convention or type identifier of the evidence associated with the security detection. For example, the @odata.type from Microsoft Graph Alerts V2 or display_name from CrowdStrike Falcon Incident Behaviors.

process.description:
Describes details about the process associated to the activity that triggered
the detection.

query.description:
Describes details about the DNS query associated to the activity that triggered
the detection.

resources.description: Describes details about the cloud resources directly related to activity that triggered the detection. For resources impacted by the detection, use Affected Resources at the top-level of the finding.

script.description:
Describes details about the script that was associated with the activity that
triggered the detection.

src_endpoint.description:
Describes details about the source of the network activity that triggered the
detection.

tls.description:
Describes details about the Transport Layer Security (TLS) activity that
triggered the detection.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the evidence associated with the security detection. For example, the activity_id from CrowdStrike Falcon Alerts or behavior_id from CrowdStrike Falcon Incident Behaviors.

url.description:
The URL object that pertains to the event or object associated to the activity
that triggered the detection.

user.description:
Describes details about the user that was the target or somehow else associated
with the activity that triggered the detection.

verdict.description: The normalized verdict of the evidence associated with the security detection.

verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict (or status) ID of the evidence associated with the security detection. For example, Microsoft Graph Security Alerts contain a verdict enumeration for each type of evidence associated with the Alert. This is typically set by an automated investigation process or an analyst/investigator assigned to the finding.

Evidences.title: Evidences

EvidencesVerdictIdEnum.description: The normalized verdict (or status) ID of the evidence associated with the security detection. For example, Microsoft Graph Security Alerts contain a verdict enumeration for each type of evidence associated with the Alert. This is typically set by an automated investigation process or an analyst/investigator assigned to the finding.
EvidencesVerdictIdEnum.title: EvidencesVerdictIdEnum

Extension.description: The OCSF Schema Extension object provides detailed information about the schema extension used to construct the event. The schema extensions are registered in the extensions.md file.

name.description: The schema extension name. For example: dev.

uid.description: The schema extension unique identifier. For example: 999.

version.description: The schema extension version. For example: 1.0.0-alpha.2.

Extension.title: Extension

FactorTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier for the authentication factor.
FactorTypeIdEnum.title: FactorTypeIdEnum

Feature.description:
The Feature object provides information about the software product feature that
generated a specific event. It encompasses details related to the capabilities,
components, user interface (UI) design, and performance upgrades associated
with the feature.

name.description: The name of the feature.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the feature.

version.description: The version of the feature.

Feature.title: Feature

File.description:
The File object represents the metadata associated with a file stored in a
computer system. It encompasses information about the file itself, including
its attributes, properties, and organizational details.

accessed_time.description: The time when the file was last accessed.

accessor.description: The name of the user who last accessed the object.

attributes.description: The bitmask value that represents the file attributes.

company_name.description: The name of the company that published the file. For example: Microsoft Corporation.

confidentiality.description:
The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value.
In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.

created_time.description: The time when the file was created.

creator.description: The user that created the file.

data_classification.description:
The Data Classification object includes information about data classification
levels and data category types.

data_classifications.description:
A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data
classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

desc.description:
The description of the file, as returned by file system. For example: the
description as returned by the Unix file command or the Windows file type.

drive_type.description: The drive type, normalized to the caption of the drive_type_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the source.

drive_type_id.description: Identifies the type of a disk drive, i.e. fixed, removable, etc.

encryption_details.description:
The encryption details of the file. Should be populated if the file is
encrypted.

ext.description: The extension of the file, excluding the leading dot. For example: exe from svchost.exe, or gz from export.tar.gz.

imported_symbols.description: A list of symbols imported by the executable file.

internal_name.description: The name of the file as identified within the file itself. This contrasts with the name by which the file is known on disk. Where available, the internal name is widely used by security practitioners and detection content because the on-disk file name is not reliable. On the Windows OS, most PE files contain a VERSIONINFO resource from which the internal name can be obtained. On macOS, binaries can optionally embed a copy of the application's Info.plist file which in turn contains the name of the executable.

is_deleted.description: Indicates if the file was deleted from the filesystem.

is_encrypted.description: Indicates if the file is encrypted.

is_public.description:
Indicates if the file is publicly accessible. For example in an object's public
access in AWS S3

is_readonly.description: Indicates that the file cannot be modified.

is_system.description: The indication of whether the object is part of the operating system.

mime_type.description:
The Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) type of the file, if
applicable.

modified_time.description: The time when the file was last modified.

modifier.description: The user that last modified the file.

name.description: The name of the file. For example: svchost.exe

owner.description: The user that owns the file/object.

parent_folder.description: The parent folder in which the file resides. For example: c:\windows\system32

path.description: The full path to the file. For example: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe.

product.description: The product that created or installed the file.

signature.description: The digital signature of the file.

size.description: The size of data, in bytes.

storage_class.description: The storage class of the file. For example in AWS S3: STANDARD, STANDARD_IA, GLACIER.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the file.

type.description: The file type.

type_id.description: The file type ID. Note the distinction between a Regular File and an Executable File. If the distinction is not known, or not indicated by the log, use Regular File. In this case, it should not be assumed that a Regular File is not executable.

uid.description:
The unique identifier of the file as defined by the storage system, such the
file system file ID.

uri.description: The file URI, such as those reporting by static analysis tools. E.g., file:///C:/dev/sarif/sarif-tutorials/samples/Introduction/simple-example.js

url.description: The URL of the file, when applicable.

version.description: The file version. For example: 8.0.7601.17514.

volume.description: The volume on the storage device where the file is located.

xattributes.description:
An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs where each pair represents a file or folder extended attribute.

For example: Windows alternate data stream attributes (ADS stream name, ADS size, etc.), user-defined or application-defined attributes, ACL, owner, primary group, etc. Examples from DCS:

  • ads_name
  • ads_size
  • dacl
  • owner
  • primary_group
  • link_name - name of the link associated to the file.
  • hard_link_count - the number of links that are associated to the file.

File.title: File

FileActivity.description:
File System Activity events report when a process performs an action on a file
or folder.

access_mask.description: The access mask in a platform-native format.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description: The actor that performed the activity on the file object

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:

associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

component.description:
The name or relative pathname of a sub-component of the data object, if applicable.

For example: attachment.doc, attachment.zip/bad.doc, or part.mime/part.cab/part.uue/part.doc.

The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the

confidence_id.description:

finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

create_mask.description: The original Windows mask that is required to create the object.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

file.description: The file that is the target of the activity.

file_diff.description:
File content differences used for change detection. For example, a common use
case is to identify itemized changes within INI or configuration/property
setting values.

file_result.description: The resulting file object when the activity was allowed and successful.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,

policy.description:

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

status.description:

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

FileActivity.title: FileActivity

FileHosting.description:
File Hosting Activity events report the actions taken by file management
applications, including file sharing servers like Sharepoint and services such
as Box, MS OneDrive, Google Drive, or network file share services.

access_list.description: The list of requested access rights.

access_mask.description: The sum of hexadecimal values of requested access rights.

access_result.description: The list of access check results.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description: The actor that performed the activity on the target file.
