FileHosting.title: FileHosting

FileQuery.description:
File Query events report information about files that are present on the
system.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in

enrichments.description:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

file.description: The file that is the target of the query.

Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

Finding.description:
The Finding event is a generic event that defines a set of attributes available
in the Findings category.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the finding activity.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

assignee.description: The details of the user assigned to an Incident.

assignee_group.description: The details of the group assigned to an Incident.

An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &

Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

comment.description: A user provided comment about the finding.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description:
Describes the affected device/host. If applicable, it can be used in conjunction with Resource(s).

e.g. Specific details about an AWS EC2 instance, that is affected by the Finding.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event included in the finding.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

finding_info.description: Describes the supporting information about a generated finding.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

impact.description:
The impact , normalized to the caption of the impact_id value. In the case of
'Other', it is defined by the event source.

impact_id.description:
The normalized impact of the incident or finding. Per NIST, this is the
magnitude of harm that can be expected to result from the consequences of
unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction, or loss of information or
information system availability.

impact_score.description: The impact as an integer value of the finding, valid range 0-100.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

is_suspected_breach.description: A determination based on analytics as to whether a potential breach was found.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

priority.description:
The priority, normalized to the caption of the priority_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

priority_id.description:
The normalized priority. Priority identifies the relative importance of the
incident or finding. It is a measurement of urgency.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_url.description: A Url link used to access the original incident.

start_time.description: The time of the least recent event included in the finding.

status.description:
The normalized status of the Finding set by the consumer normalized to the
caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the
source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the Finding, set by the consumer.

ticket.description: The linked ticket in the ticketing system.

tickets.description:
The associated ticket(s) in the ticketing system. Each ticket contains details
like ticket ID, status, etc.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.

verdict.description: The verdict assigned to an Incident finding.

verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict of an Incident.

Finding.title: Finding

FindingActivityIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the finding activity.
FindingActivityIdEnum.title: FindingActivityIdEnum

FindingInfo.description:
The Finding Information object describes metadata related to a security finding
generated by a security tool or system.

analytic.description:
The analytic technique used to analyze and derive insights from the data or
information that led to the finding or conclusion.

attack_graph.description:
An Attack Graph describes possible routes an attacker could take through an
environment. It describes relationships between resources and their findings,
such as malware detections, vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and other
security actions.

attacks.description: The MITRE ATT&CK® technique and associated tactics related to the finding.

created_time.description: The time when the finding was created.

data_sources.description: A list of data sources utilized in generation of the finding.

desc.description: The description of the reported finding.

first_seen_time.description:
The time when the finding was first observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was first observed.

It can differ from the created_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was created.

kill_chain.description: The Cyber Kill Chain® provides a detailed description of each phase and its associated activities within the broader context of a cyber attack.

last_seen_time.description:
The time when the finding was most recently observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was most recently observed.

It can differ from the modified_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was last modified.

modified_time.description: The time when the finding was last modified.

product.description: Details about the product that reported the finding.

product_uid.description: The unique identifier of the product that reported the finding.

related_analytics.description: Other analytics related to this finding.

related_events.description:
Describes events and/or other findings related to the finding as identified by
the security product. Note that these events may or may not be in OCSF.

related_events_count.description: Number of related events or findings.

src_url.description: The URL pointing to the source of the finding.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated with the finding.

title.description: A title or a brief phrase summarizing the reported finding.

traits.description: The list of key traits or characteristics extracted from the finding.

types.description: One or more types of the reported finding.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the reported finding.

uid_alt.description: The alternative unique identifier of the reported finding.

FindingInfo.title: FindingInfo

FindingObject.description:
The Finding object describes metadata related to a security finding generated
by a security tool or system.

desc.description: The description of the reported finding.

modified_time.description: The time when the finding was last modified.

related_events.description:

the security product. Note that these events may or may not be in OCSF.

remediation.description: Describes the recommended remediation steps to address identified issue(s).

supporting_data.description: Additional data supporting a finding as provided by security tool

uid.description: The unique identifier of the reported finding.

FindingStatusIdEnum.description: The normalized status identifier of the Finding, set by the consumer.

Fingerprint.description:
The Fingerprint object provides detailed information about a fingerprint, which
is a compact representation of data used to identify a longer piece of
information, such as a public key, file content, or application implementation.
It contains the algorithm or scheme and value of the fingerprint, enabling
efficient and reliable identification of the associated data.

algorithm.description: The algorithm or scheme used to create the fingerprint, normalized to the caption of algorithm_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

algorithm_id.description:
The identifier of the normalized algorithm or scheme, which was used to create
the fingerprint.

value.description:
The fingerprint value.

Note: This uses type file_hash_t ("Hash"), which has been generalized for all fingerprints but retains the same name and caption for backwards compatibility.

Fingerprint.title: Fingerprint

FingerprintAlgorithmIdEnum.description:
The identifier of the normalized algorithm or scheme, which was used to create
the fingerprint.
FingerprintAlgorithmIdEnum.title: FingerprintAlgorithmIdEnum

FirewallRule.description:
The Firewall Rule object represents a specific rule within a firewall policy or
event. It contains information about a rule's configuration, properties, and
associated actions that define how network traffic is handled by the firewall.

condition.description: The rule trigger condition for the rule. For example: SQL_INJECTION.

desc.description: The description of the rule that generated the event.

duration.description: The rule response time duration, usually used for challenge completion time.

match_details.description: The data in a request that rule matched. For example: '["10","and","1"]'.

match_location.description:
The location of the matched data in the source which resulted in the triggered
firewall rule. For example: HEADER.

name.description: The name of the rule that generated the event.

rate_limit.description: The rate limit for a rate-based rule.

sensitivity.description: The sensitivity of the firewall rule in the matched event. For example: HIGH.

type.description: The rule type.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the rule that generated the event.

version.description: The rule version. For example: 1.1.

FirewallRule.title: FirewallRule

usage.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

folder.description: The folder that is the target of the query.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

query_info.description: The search details associated with the query request.

query_result_id.description: The normalized identifier of the query result.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

FolderQuery.title: FolderQuery

FormatIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the format. See specific usage.
FormatIdEnum.title: FormatIdEnum

FtpActivity.description:
File Transfer Protocol (FTP) Activity events report file transfers between a
server and a client as seen on the network.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

app_name.description:
The network application name identified by tools such as NBAR or App ID (e.g.,
youtube, facebook, webex). This represents a specific network application that
uses standard protocols (such as https or quic) to deliver its service.

app_protocol_name.description:
The application-layer (Layer 7) protocol name identified by deep packet inspection or packet parsing (e.g., https, quic, ssh, dns), expressed as an IANA-registered service name from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number Registry.

Note: Port numbers alone are not always a reliable indicator of the actual application protocol in use.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

codes.description: The list of return codes to the FTP command.

command_responses.description: The list of responses to the FTP command.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

cumulative_traffic.description: The cumulative (running total) network traffic aggregated from the start of a flow or session. Use when reporting: (1) total accumulated bytes/packets since flow initiation, (2) combined aggregation models where both incremental deltas and running totals are reported together (populate both traffic for the delta and this attribute for the cumulative total), or (3) final summary metrics when a long-lived connection closes. This represents the sum of all activity from flow start to the current observation, not a delta or point-in-time value.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description: The responder (server) in a network connection.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

file.description: The file that is the target of the FTP activity.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

ja4_fingerprint_list.description: A list of the JA4+ network fingerprints.

load_balancer.description:
The Load Balancer object contains information related to the device that is
distributing incoming traffic to specified destinations.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

name.description: The name of the data affiliated with the command.

network_observation_point.description: The network endpoint that observes or inspects network traffic as a third-party system, used when the observer is neither the source nor the destination of the communication (when observation_point_id = 3). Examples include network taps, span ports, inline security devices, or packet capture systems that monitor traffic between other endpoints.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

observation_point.description: Indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity. The value is normalized to the caption of the observation_point_id.

observation_point_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the observation point. The observation point
identifier indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network
endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

packet_list.description: The list of packet objects describing captured network packets.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

port.description: The dynamic port established for impending data transfers.

proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_connection_info.description: The connection information from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_endpoint.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_http_request.description: The HTTP Request from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_http_response.description: The HTTP Response from the remote server to the proxy server.

proxy_tls.description: The TLS protocol negotiated between the proxy server and the remote server.

proxy_traffic.description:
The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from
proxy to remote server at a given point of time.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description: The initiator (client) of the network connection.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.

type.description: The type of FTP network connection (e.g. active, passive).

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

FtpActivity.title: FtpActivity

FunctionInvocation.description:
The Function Invocation object provides details regarding the invocation of a
function.

error.description: The error indication returned from the function. This may differ from the return value (e.g. when errno is used).

parameters.description: The parameters passed into a function invocation.

return_value.description: The value returned from a function.

FunctionInvocation.title: FunctionInvocation

GpuInfo.description:
The GPU information object contains attributes describing graphical processing
hardware.

bus_type.description:
The attachment bus or interface standard, normalized to the caption of the
bus_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

bus_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the attachment bus or interface standard.

cores.description: The number of processing cores or compute units for the component.

model.description: The model name of the GPU. For example: GeForce RTX A6000, Radeon PRO W7900, or Intel Data Center GPU Max 1550.

vendor_name.description: The name of the vendor of the GPU. For example: NVIDIA, AMD, or Intel.

vram_mode.description:
The video memory attachment mode, indicating how the VRAM hardware is
integrated with the system (e.g., shared or dedicated), normalized to the
caption of the vram_mode_id value. For 'Other', the exact attachment mode is
defined by the event source.

vram_mode_id.description: The normalized identifier of the video memory attachment mode.

vram_size.description: The total amount of installed video RAM.

GpuInfo.title: GpuInfo

GpuInfoBusTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the attachment bus or interface standard.
GpuInfoBusTypeIdEnum.title: GpuInfoBusTypeIdEnum

Graph.description: A graph data structure representation with nodes and edges.

desc.description:
The graph description - provides additional details about the graph's purpose
and contents.

edges.description: The edges/connections between nodes in the graph - contains the collection of edge objects defining relationships between nodes.

is_directed.description: Indicates if the graph is directed (true) or undirected (false).

name.description: The graph name - a human readable identifier for the graph.

nodes.description: The nodes/vertices of the graph - contains the collection of node objects that make up the graph.

query_language.description: The graph query language, normalized to the caption of the query_language_id value.

query_language_id.description:
The normalized identifier of a graph query language that can be used to
interact with the graph.

type.description:
The graph type. Typically useful to represent the specific type of graph that
is used.

uid.description: Unique identifier of the graph - a unique ID to reference this specific graph.

Graph.title: Graph

GraphQueryLanguageIdEnum.description:
The normalized identifier of a graph query language that can be used to
interact with the graph.
GraphQueryLanguageIdEnum.title: GraphQueryLanguageIdEnum

Group.description:
The Group object represents a collection or association of entities, such as
users, policies, or devices. It serves as a logical grouping mechanism to
organize and manage entities with similar characteristics or permissions within
a system or organization, including but not limited to purposes of access
control.

desc.description: The group description.

domain.description:
The domain where the group is defined. For example: the LDAP or Active
Directory domain.

name.description: The group name.

type.description: The type of the group.

uid.description:
The unique identifier of the group. For example, for Windows events this is the
security identifier (SID) of the group. Another example, pool id or desktop id
that the device belongs to.

Group.title: Group

GroupManagement.description:
Group Management events report management updates to a group, including updates
to membership and permissions.

Hassh.description:
The HASSH object contains SSH network fingerprinting values for specific
client/server implementations. It provides a standardized way of identifying
and categorizing SSH connections based on their unique characteristics and
behavior.

algorithm.description:
The concatenation of key exchange, encryption, authentication and compression
algorithms (separated by ';'). NOTE: This is not the underlying algorithm for
the hash implementation.

fingerprint.description:
The hash of the key exchange, encryption, authentication and compression
algorithms.

Hassh.title: Hassh

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

network_observation_point.description: The network endpoint that observes or inspects network traffic as a third-party system, used when the observer is neither the source nor the destination of the communication (when observation_point_id = 3). Examples include network taps, span ports, inline security devices, or packet capture systems that monitor traffic between other endpoints.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

observation_point.description: Indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity. The value is normalized to the caption of the observation_point_id.

observation_point_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the observation point. The observation point
identifier indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network
endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

packet_list.description: The list of packet objects describing captured network packets.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_connection_info.description: The connection information from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_endpoint.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_http_request.description: The HTTP Request from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_http_response.description: The HTTP Response from the remote server to the proxy server.

proxy_tls.description: The TLS protocol negotiated between the proxy server and the remote server.

proxy_traffic.description:
The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from
proxy to remote server at a given point of time.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description: The initiator (client) of the network connection.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

trace.description:
The trace object contains information about distributed traces which are
critical to observability and describe how requests move through a system,
capturing each step's timing and status.

traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

HttpActivity.title: HttpActivity

HttpCookie.description:
The HTTP Cookie object, also known as a web cookie or browser cookie, contains
details and values pertaining to a small piece of data that a server sends to a
user's web browser. This data is then stored by the browser and sent back to
the server with subsequent requests, allowing the server to remember and track
certain information about the user's browsing session or preferences.

domain.description: The domain name for the server from which the http_cookie is served.

expiration_time.description: The expiration time of the HTTP cookie.

http_only.description: A cookie attribute to make it inaccessible via JavaScript

is_http_only.description: This attribute prevents the cookie from being accessed via JavaScript.

is_secure.description:
The cookie attribute indicates that cookies are sent to the server only when
the request is encrypted using the HTTPS protocol.

name.description: The HTTP cookie name.

path.description: The path of the HTTP cookie.

samesite.description:
The cookie attribute that lets servers specify whether/when cookies are sent
with cross-site requests. Values are: Strict, Lax or None

secure.description:
The cookie attribute to only send cookies to the server with an encrypted
request over the HTTPS protocol.

HttpCookie.title: HttpCookie

HttpHeader.description:
The HTTP Header object represents the headers sent in an HTTP request or
response. HTTP headers are key-value pairs that convey additional information
about the HTTP message, including details about the content, caching,
authentication, encoding, and other aspects of the communication.

name.description: The name of the HTTP header.

value.description: The value of the HTTP header.

HttpHeader.title: HttpHeader

HttpRequest.description:
The HTTP Request object represents the attributes of a request made to a web
server. It encapsulates the details and metadata associated with an HTTP
request, including the request method, headers, URL, query parameters, body
content, and other relevant information.

args.description: The arguments sent along with the HTTP request.

body_length.description:
The actual length of the HTTP request body, in number of bytes, independent of
a potentially existing Content-Length header.

http_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers of an HTTP request or response.

http_method.description: The HTTP request method indicates the desired action to be performed for a given resource.

length.description: The length of the entire HTTP request, in number of bytes.

referrer.description:
The request header that identifies the address of the previous web page, which
is linked to the current web page or resource being requested.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the http request.

url.description: The URL object that pertains to the request.

user_agent.description: The request header that identifies the operating system and web browser.

version.description: The Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) version.

x_forwarded_for.description:
The X-Forwarded-For header identifying the originating IP address(es) of a
client connecting to a web server through an HTTP proxy or a load balancer.

HttpRequest.title: HttpRequest

HttpRequestHttpMethodEnum.description: The HTTP request method indicates the desired action to be performed for a given resource.
HttpRequestHttpMethodEnum.title: HttpRequestHttpMethodEnum

HttpResponse.description:
The HTTP Response object contains detailed information about the response sent
from a web server to the requester. It encompasses attributes and metadata that
describe the response status, headers, body content, and other relevant
information.

body_length.description:
The actual length of the HTTP response body, in number of bytes, independent of
a potentially existing Content-Length header.

code.description:
The Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) status code returned from the web server
to the client. For example, 200.

content_type.description: The request header that identifies the original media type of the resource (prior to any content encoding applied for sending).

http_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers of an HTTP request or response.

latency.description: The HTTP response latency measured in milliseconds.

length.description: The length of the entire HTTP response, in number of bytes.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

status.description:
The response status. For example: A successful HTTP status of 'OK' which
corresponds to a code of 200.

HttpResponse.title: HttpResponse

IamAnalysisFinding.description:
This finding represents an IAM analysis result, which evaluates IAM policies, access patterns, and IAM configurations for potential security risks. The analysis can focus on either an identity (user, role, service account) or a resource to assess permissions, access patterns, and security posture within the IAM domain.
Note: Use permission_analysis_results for identity-centric analysis (evaluating what an identity can do) and access_analysis_result for resource-centric analysis (evaluating who can access a resource). These complement each other for comprehensive IAM security assessment.
Note: If the Finding is an incident, i.e. requires incident workflow, also apply the incident profile or aggregate this finding into an Incident Finding.

access_analysis_result.description:
Describes access relationships and pathways between identities, resources,
focusing on who can access what and through which mechanisms. This evaluates
access levels (read/write/admin), access types (direct, cross-account, public,
federated), and the conditions under which access is granted. Use this for
resource-centric security assessments such as external access discovery, public
exposure analysis, etc.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the finding activity.

activity_name.description: The finding activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

applications.description:
Details about applications, services, or systems that are accessible based on
the IAM analysis. For identity-centric analysis, this represents applications
the identity can access. For resource-centric analysis, this represents
applications that can access the resource.

assignee.description: The details of the user assigned to an Incident.

assignee_group.description: The details of the group assigned to an Incident.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

comment.description: A user provided comment about the finding.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description:
Describes the affected device/host. If applicable, it can be used in conjunction with Resource(s).

e.g. Specific details about an AWS EC2 instance, that is affected by the Finding.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event included in the finding.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

finding_info.description: Describes the supporting information about a generated finding.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

identity_activity_metrics.description:
Describes usage activity and other metrics of an Identity i.e. AWS IAM User,
GCP IAM Principal, etc.

impact.description:
The impact , normalized to the caption of the impact_id value. In the case of
'Other', it is defined by the event source.

impact_id.description:
The normalized impact of the incident or finding. Per NIST, this is the
magnitude of harm that can be expected to result from the consequences of
unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction, or loss of information or
information system availability.

impact_score.description: The impact as an integer value of the finding, valid range 0-100.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

is_suspected_breach.description: A determination based on analytics as to whether a potential breach was found.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

permission_analysis_results.description:
Describes analysis results of permissions, policies directly associated with an
identity (user, role, or service account). This evaluates what permissions an
identity has been granted through attached policies, which privileges are
actively used versus unused, and identifies potential over-privileged access.
Use this for identity-centric security assessments such as privilege audits,
dormant permission discovery, and least-privilege compliance analysis.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

priority.description:
The priority, normalized to the caption of the priority_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

priority_id.description:
The normalized priority. Priority identifies the relative importance of the
incident or finding. It is a measurement of urgency.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

remediation.description: Describes the recommended remediation steps to address identified issue(s).

resources.description:
Details about resources involved in the IAM analysis. For identity-centric
analysis, this represents resources the identity can access. For
resource-centric analysis, this represents the resource being analyzed and
related resources in the access chain.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_url.description: A Url link used to access the original incident.

start_time.description: The time of the least recent event included in the finding.

status.description:
The normalized status of the Finding set by the consumer normalized to the
caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the
source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the Finding, set by the consumer.

ticket.description: The linked ticket in the ticketing system.

tickets.description:
The associated ticket(s) in the ticketing system. Each ticket contains details
like ticket ID, status, etc.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

user.description:
Details about the identity (user, role, service account, or other principal)
that is the subject of the IAM analysis. This provides context about the
identity being evaluated for security risks and access patterns.

vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.

verdict.description: The verdict assigned to an Incident finding.

verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict of an Incident.

IamAnalysisFinding.title: IamAnalysisFinding

IamEvent.description:
The Identity & Access Management event is a generic event that defines a set of
attributes available in the access control events. As a generic event, it could
be used to log events that are not otherwise defined by the IAM category.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

http_request.description: Details about the underlying HTTP request.

http_response.description: Details about the underlying HTTP response.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description: Details about the source of the IAM activity.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:

IamEvent.title: IamEvent

IdentityActivityMetrics.description:
The Identity Activity Metrics object captures usage patterns, authentication
activity, credential usage and other metrics for identities across cloud and
on-premises environments. Example identities include AWS IAM Users, Roles,
Azure AD Principals, GCP Service Accounts, on-premises Active Directory
accounts.

first_seen_time.description:
The timestamp when this identity was first observed or created in the system.
This helps establish the identity's age and lifecycle stage for risk
assessment.

last_authentication_time.description: The timestamp when this identity last successfully authenticated to any system or service. This differs from last_seen_time as it specifically tracks authentication events rather than all activities.

last_seen_time.description:
The timestamp of the most recent activity performed by this identity, including
authentication, resource access, or API calls. This is the most comprehensive
indicator of identity usage recency.

password_last_used_time.description:
The timestamp when password-based authentication was last used by this
identity. This helps distinguish between password and other authentication
methods (MFA, SSO, certificates) and identify password-specific usage patterns.

programmatic_credentials.description:
Details about the programmatic credentials associated with this identity, such
as API keys, service account keys, access tokens, and client certificates used
for automated access.

IdentityActivityMetrics.title: IdentityActivityMetrics

Idp.description:
The Identity Provider object contains detailed information about a provider
responsible for creating, maintaining, and managing identity information while
offering authentication services to applications. An Identity Provider (IdP)
serves as a trusted authority that verifies the identity of users and issues
authentication tokens or assertions to enable secure access to applications or
services.

auth_factors.description:
The Authentication Factors object describes the different types of Multi-Factor
Authentication (MFA) methods and/or devices supported by the Identity Provider.

domain.description: The primary domain associated with the Identity Provider.

fingerprint.description: The fingerprint of the X.509 certificate used by the Identity Provider.

has_mfa.description: The Identity Provider enforces Multi Factor Authentication (MFA).

issuer.description:
The unique identifier (often a URL) used by the Identity Provider as its
issuer.

name.description: The name of the Identity Provider.

protocol_name.description: The supported protocol of the Identity Provider. E.g., SAML, OIDC, or OAuth2.

scim.description: The System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) resource object provides a structured set of attributes related to SCIM protocols used for identity provisioning and management across cloud-based platforms. It standardizes user and group provisioning details, enabling identity synchronization and lifecycle management with compatible Identity Providers (IdPs) and applications. SCIM is defined in RFC-7634

sso.description:
The Single Sign-On (SSO) object provides a structure for normalizing SSO
attributes, configuration, and/or settings from Identity Providers.

state.description: The configuration state of the Identity Provider, normalized to the caption of the state_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

state_id.description:
The normalized state ID of the Identity Provider to reflect its configuration
or activation status.

tenant_uid.description: The tenant ID associated with the Identity Provider.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the Identity Provider.

url_string.description: The URL for accessing the configuration or metadata of the Identity Provider.

Idp.title: Idp

IdpStateIdEnum.description:
The normalized state ID of the Identity Provider to reflect its configuration
or activation status.
IdpStateIdEnum.title: IdpStateIdEnum

Image.description:
The Image object provides a description of a specific Virtual Machine (VM) or
Container image.

labels.description: The list of labels associated to the image.

name.description: The image name. For example: elixir.

path.description: The full path to the image file.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the image.

uid.description: The unique image ID. For example: 77af4d6b9913.

Image.title: Image

ImpactIdEnum.description:
The normalized impact of the incident or finding. Per NIST, this is the
magnitude of harm that can be expected to result from the consequences of
unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction, or loss of information or
information system availability.
ImpactIdEnum.title: ImpactIdEnum

IncidentFinding.description:
An Incident Finding reports the creation, update, or closure of security incidents as a result of detections and/or analytics.
Note: Incident Finding implicitly includes the incident profile and it should be added to the metadata.profiles[] array.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the Incident activity.

activity_name.description: The Incident activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

assignee.description: The details of the user assigned to an Incident.

assignee_group.description: The details of the group assigned to an Incident.

attacks.description: An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing the tactics, techniques & sub-techniques associated to the Incident.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

comment.description: Additional user supplied details for updating or closing the incident.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

desc.description: The short description of the Incident.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event included in the incident.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

finding_info_list.description: A list of finding_info objects associated to an incident.

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

impact.description:
The impact , normalized to the caption of the impact_id value. In the case of
'Other', it is defined by the event source.

impact_id.description:
The normalized impact of the incident or finding. Per NIST, this is the
magnitude of harm that can be expected to result from the consequences of
unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction, or loss of information or
information system availability.

impact_score.description: The impact as an integer value of the finding, valid range 0-100.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

is_suspected_breach.description: A determination based on analytics as to whether a potential breach was found.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

priority.description:
The priority, normalized to the caption of the priority_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

priority_id.description:
The normalized priority. Priority identifies the relative importance of the
incident or finding. It is a measurement of urgency.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_url.description: A Url link used to access the original incident.

start_time.description: The time of the least recent event included in the incident.

status.description:
The normalized status of the Incident normalized to the caption of the
status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the Incident.

ticket.description: The linked ticket in the ticketing system.

tickets.description:
The associated ticket(s) in the ticketing system. Each ticket contains details
like ticket ID, status, etc.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.

verdict.description: The verdict assigned to an Incident finding.

verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict of an Incident.

IncidentFinding.title: IncidentFinding

IncidentFindingActivityIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the Incident activity.
IncidentFindingActivityIdEnum.title: IncidentFindingActivityIdEnum

IncidentFindingStatusIdEnum.description: The normalized status identifier of the Incident.
IncidentFindingStatusIdEnum.title: IncidentFindingStatusIdEnum

InitiatorIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the initiator. See specific usage.
InitiatorIdEnum.title: InitiatorIdEnum

InjectionTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the process injection method.
InjectionTypeIdEnum.title: InjectionTypeIdEnum

InstallStateIdEnum.description: The normalized state of the install.
InstallStateIdEnum.title: InstallStateIdEnum

IntegrityIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the process integrity level (Windows only).
IntegrityIdEnum.title: IntegrityIdEnum

InventoryInfo.description:
Device Inventory Info events report device inventory data that is either logged
or proactively collected. For example, when collecting device information from
a CMDB or running a network sweep of connected devices.
