Ja4Fingerprint.description:
The JA4+ fingerprint object provides detailed fingerprint information about
various aspects of network traffic which is both machine and human readable.

section_a.description: The 'a' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

section_b.description: The 'b' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

section_c.description: The 'c' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

section_d.description: The 'd' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

type.description: The JA4+ fingerprint type as defined by FoxIO, normalized to the caption of 'type_id'. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

type_id.description: The identifier of the JA4+ fingerprint type.

Ja4Fingerprint.title: Ja4Fingerprint

Ja4FingerprintTypeIdEnum.description: The identifier of the JA4+ fingerprint type.
Ja4FingerprintTypeIdEnum.title: Ja4FingerprintTypeIdEnum

Job.description:
The Job object provides information about a scheduled job or task, including
its name, command line, and state. It encompasses attributes that describe the
properties and status of the scheduled job.

created_time.description: The time when the job was created.

desc.description: The description of the job.

file.description: The file that pertains to the job.

last_run_time.description: The time when the job was last run.

name.description: The name of the job.

next_run_time.description: The time when the job will next be run.

run_state.description: The run state of the job.

run_state_id.description: The run state ID of the job.

user.description: The user that created the job.

Job.title: Job

JobQuery.description: Job Query events report information about scheduled jobs.

KbArticle.description: The KB Article object contains metadata that describes the patch or update.

classification.description: The vendors classification of the kb article.

created_time.description: The date the kb article was released by the vendor.

install_state.description: The install state of the kb article.

install_state_id.description: The normalized install state ID of the kb article.

is_superseded.description: The kb article has been replaced by another.

os.description: The operating system the kb article applies.

product.description: The product details the kb article applies.

severity.description: The severity of the kb article.

size.description: The size in bytes for the kb article.

src_url.description: The kb article link from the source vendor.

title.description: The title of the kb article.

uid.description: The unique identifier for the kb article.

KbArticle.title: KbArticle

Kernel.description:
The Kernel Resource object provides information about a specific kernel
resource, including its name and type. It describes essential attributes
associated with a resource managed by the kernel of an operating system.

is_system.description: The indication of whether the object is part of the operating system.

name.description: The name of the kernel resource.

path.description: The full path of the kernel resource.

system_call.description: The system call that was invoked.

type.description: The type of the kernel resource.

type_id.description: The type of the kernel resource.

Kernel.title: Kernel

KernelActivity.description:
Kernel Activity events report when an process creates, reads, or deletes a
kernel resource.

of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

KernelActivity.title: KernelActivity

KernelDriver.description:
The Kernel Extension object describes a kernel driver that has been loaded or
unloaded into the operating system (OS) kernel.

file.description: The driver/extension file object.

KernelDriver.title: KernelDriver

KernelExtensionActivity.description:
Kernel Extension events report when a driver/extension is loaded or unloaded
into the kernel

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description: The actor process that loaded or unloaded the driver/extension.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

driver.description: The driver that was loaded/unloaded into the kernel

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

KernelExtensionActivity.title: KernelExtensionActivity

KernelObjectQuery.description:
Kernel Object Query events report information about discovered kernel
resources.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

KernelTypeIdEnum.description: The type of the kernel resource.
KernelTypeIdEnum.title: KernelTypeIdEnum

KeyValueObject.description: A generic object allowing to define a {key:value} pair.

name.description: The name of the key.

value.description: The value associated to the key.

values.description:
Optional, the values associated to the key. You can populate this attribute,
when you have multiple values for the same key.

KeyValueObject.title: KeyValueObject

KeyboardInfo.description:
The Keyboard Information object contains details and attributes related to a
computer or device keyboard. It encompasses information that describes the
characteristics, capabilities, and configuration of the keyboard.

function_keys.description: The number of function keys on client keyboard.

ime.description: The Input Method Editor (IME) file name.

keyboard_layout.description: The keyboard locale identifier name (e.g., en-US).

KeyboardInfo.title: KeyboardInfo

KillChainPhase.description: The Kill Chain Phase object represents a single phase of a cyber attack, including the initial reconnaissance and planning stages up to the final objective of the attacker. It provides a detailed description of each phase and its associated activities within the broader context of a cyber attack. See Cyber Kill Chain®.

phase_id.description: The cyber kill chain phase identifier.

KillChainPhase.title: KillChainPhase

LaunchTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier for the specific type of Launch activity.
LaunchTypeIdEnum.title: LaunchTypeIdEnum

LdapPerson.description: The additional LDAP attributes that describe a person.

cost_center.description: The cost center associated with the user.

created_time.description: The timestamp when the user was created.

deleted_time.description:
The timestamp when the user was deleted. In Active Directory (AD), when a user
is deleted they are moved to a temporary container and then removed after 30
days. So, this field can be populated even after a user is deleted for the next
30 days.

department.description: The name of the department in which the user works.

display_name.description:
The display name of the LDAP person. According to RFC 2798, this is the
preferred name of a person to be used when displaying entries.

email_addrs.description: A list of additional email addresses for the user.

employee_uid.description: The employee identifier assigned to the user by the organization.

given_name.description: The given or first name of the user.

hire_time.description: The timestamp when the user was or will be hired by the organization.

labels.description: The labels associated with the user. For example in AD this could be the userType, employeeType. For example: Member, Employee.

last_login_time.description: The last time when the user logged in.

ldap_cn.description: The LDAP and X.500 commonName attribute, typically the full name of the person. For example, John Doe.

ldap_dn.description: The X.500 Distinguished Name (DN) is a structured string that uniquely identifies an entry, such as a user, in an X.500 directory service For example, cn=John Doe,ou=People,dc=example,dc=com.

leave_time.description: The timestamp when the user left or will be leaving the organization.

location.description:
The geographical location associated with a user. This is typically the user's
usual work location.

manager.description:
The user's manager. This helps in understanding an org hierarchy. This should
only ever be populated once in an event. I.e. there should not be a manager's
manager in an event.

modified_time.description: The timestamp when the user entry was last modified.

office_location.description: The primary office location associated with the user. This could be any string and isn't a specific address. For example, South East Virtual.

phone_number.description: The telephone number of the user. Corresponds to the LDAP Telephone-Number CN.

surname.description: The last or family name for the user.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the user.

LdapPerson.title: LdapPerson

LinuxProcess.description: Extends the process object to add Linux specific fields

ancestry.description: An array of Process Entities describing the extended parentage of this process object. Direct parent information should be expressed through the parent_process attribute. The first array element is the direct parent of this process object. Subsequent list elements go up the process parentage hierarchy. That is, the array is sorted from newest to oldest process. It is recommended to only populate this field for the top-level process object.

auid.description: The audit user assigned at login by the audit subsystem.

cmd_line.description: The full command line used to launch an application, service, process, or job. For example: ssh user@10.0.0.10. If the command line is unavailable or missing, the empty string '' is to be used.

container.description:
The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a
prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system
using a container runtime like containerd.

cpid.description:
A unique process identifier that can be assigned deterministically by multiple
system data producers.

created_time.description: The time when the process was created/started.

egid.description: The effective group under which this process is running.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables associated with the process.

euid.description: The effective user under which this process is running.

file.description: The process file object.

group.description: The group under which this process is running.

integrity.description:
The process integrity level, normalized to the caption of the integrity_id
value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source (Windows
only).

integrity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the process integrity level (Windows only).

lineage.description: The lineage of the process, represented by a list of paths for each ancestor process. For example: ['/usr/sbin/sshd', '/usr/bin/bash', '/usr/bin/whoami'].

loaded_modules.description: The list of loaded module names.

name.description: The friendly name of the process, for example: Notepad++.

namespace_pid.description:
If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process
identifier within that process namespace.

parent_process.description: The parent process of this process object. It is recommended to only populate this field for the top-level process object, to prevent deep nesting. Additional ancestry information can be supplied in the ancestry attribute.

pid.description:
The process identifier, as reported by the operating system. Process ID (PID)
is a number used by the operating system to uniquely identify an active
process.

ptid.description:
The identifier of the process thread associated with the event, as returned by
the operating system.

sandbox.description:
The name of the containment jail (i.e., sandbox). For example, hardened_ps,
high_security_ps, oracle_ps, netsvcs_ps, or default_ps.

session.description: The user session under which this process is running.

terminated_time.description: The time when the process was terminated.

tid.description:
The identifier of the thread associated with the event, as returned by the
operating system.

uid.description:
A unique identifier for this process assigned by the producer (tool).
Facilitates correlation of a process event with other events for that process.

user.description: The user under which this process is running.

working_directory.description: The working directory of a process.

xattributes.description:
An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs that represent a
process extended attribute.

LinuxProcess.title: LinuxProcess

LoadBalancer.description:
The load balancer object describes the load balancer entity and contains
additional information regarding the distribution of traffic across a network.

classification.description: The request classification as defined by the load balancer.

code.description:
The numeric response status code detailing the connection from the load
balancer to the destination target.

dst_endpoint.description: The destination to which the load balancer is distributing traffic.

endpoint_connections.description: An object detailing the load balancer connection attempts and responses.

ip.description: The IP address of the load balancer node that handled the client request. Note: the load balancer may have other IP addresses, and this is not an IP address of the target/distribution endpoint - see dst_endpoint.

message.description: The load balancer message.

metrics.description: General purpose metrics associated with the load balancer.

name.description: The name of the load balancer.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional status information about the load
balancer distribution event.

uid.description: The unique identifier for the load balancer.

LoadBalancer.title: LoadBalancer

LoadTypeIdEnum.description: The normalized identifier of the load type. See specific usage.
LoadTypeIdEnum.title: LoadTypeIdEnum

Location.description:
The Geo Location object describes a geographical location, usually associated
with an IP address.

aerial_height.description: Expressed as either height above takeoff location or height above ground level (AGL) for a UAS current location. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.

coordinates.description: A two-element array, containing a longitude/latitude pair. The format conforms with GeoJSON. For example: [-73.983, 40.719].

country.description:
The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2 country code.

Note: The two letter country code should be capitalized. For example: US or CA.

desc.description: The description of the geographical location.

geodetic_altitude.description: The aircraft distance above or below the ellipsoid as measured along a line that passes through the aircraft and is normal to the surface of the WGS-84 ellipsoid. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.

geodetic_vertical_accuracy.description:
Provides quality/containment on geodetic altitude. This is based on ADS-B
Geodetic Vertical Accuracy (GVA). Measured in meters.

geohash.description:
Geohash of the geo-coordinates (latitude and longitude).

Geohashing is a geocoding system used to encode geographic coordinates in decimal degrees, to a single string.

horizontal_accuracy.description:
Provides quality/containment on horizontal position. This is based on ADS-B
NACp. Measured in meters.

is_on_premises.description: Indicates whether the location is on-premises.

isp.description: The name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP).

lat.description: The geographical Latitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: 42.361145.

long.description: The geographical Longitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: -71.057083.

postal_code.description: The postal code of the location.

pressure_altitude.description: The uncorrected barometric pressure altitude (based on reference standard 29.92 inHg, 1013.25 mb) provides a reference for algorithms that utilize 'altitude deltas' between aircraft. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m.. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.

provider.description: The provider of the geographical location data.

region.description:
The alphanumeric code that identifies the principal subdivision (e.g. province
or state) of the country. For example, 'CH-VD' for the Canton of Vaud,
Switzerland

Location.title: Location

Logger.description:
The Logger object represents the device and product where events are stored
with times for receipt and transmission.  This may be at the source device
where the event occurred, a remote scanning device, intermediate hops, or the
ultimate destination.

device.description: The device where the events are logged.

event_uid.description: The unique identifier of the event assigned by the logger.

is_truncated.description: Indicates whether the OCSF event data has been truncated due to size limitations. When true, some event data may have been omitted to fit within system constraints.

log_format.description: The format of data in the log. For example JSON, syslog or CSV.

log_level.description:
The level at which an event was logged. This can be log provider specific. For
example the audit level.

log_name.description:
The log name for the logging provider log, or the file name of the system log.
This may be an intermediate store-and-forward log or a vendor destination log.
For example /archive/server1/var/log/messages.0 or /var/log/.

log_provider.description:
The logging provider or logging service that logged the event. This may be an
intermediate application store-and-forward log or a vendor destination log.

log_version.description:
The event log schema version of the original event. For example the syslog
version or the Cisco Log Schema version

logged_time.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.

name.description: The name of the logging product instance.

product.description:
The product logging the event.  This may be the event source product, a
management server product, a scanning product, a SIEM, etc.

transmit_time.description:
The time when the event was transmitted from the logging device to it's next
destination.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the logging product instance.

untruncated_size.description: The original size of the OCSF event data in kilobytes before any truncation occurred. This field is typically populated when is_truncated is true to indicate the full size of the original event.

version.description: The version of the logging provider.

Logger.title: Logger

LongString.description:
This object is a used to capture strings which may be truncated by a security
product due to their length.

is_truncated.description: Indicates that value has been truncated. May be omitted if truncation has not occurred.

untruncated_size.description: The size in bytes of the string represented by value before truncation. Should be omitted if truncation has not occurred.

value.description: The string value, truncated if is_truncated is true.

LongString.title: LongString

uid.description:
A unique identifier for this process assigned by the producer (tool).
Facilitates correlation of a process event with other events for that process.

user.description: The user under which this process is running.

working_directory.description: The working directory of a process.

xattributes.description:
An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs that represent a
process extended attribute.

MacosProcess.title: MacosProcess

Malware.description:
The Malware object describes the classification of known malicious software,
which is intentionally designed to cause damage to a computer, server, client,
or computer network.

classification_ids.description: The list of normalized identifiers of the malware classifications.

classifications.description: The list of malware classifications, normalized to the captions of the classification_ids values. In the case of 'Other', they are defined by the event source.

cves.description: The list of Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) identifiers associated with the malware. Reference: CVE

files.description:
The list of file objects representing files that were identified as infected by
the malware.

name.description: The malware name, as reported by the detection engine.

num_infected.description: The number of files that were identified to be infected by the malware.

path.description: The filesystem path of the malware that was observed.

provider.description:
The name or identifier of the security solution or service that provided the
malware detection information.

severity.description: The severity of the malware, normalized to the captions of the severity_id values. In the case of 'Other', they are defined by the event source.

severity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the malware severity.

uid.description:
A unique identifier for the specific malware instance, as assigned by the
detection engine (e.g., virus signature ID or IPS rule ID).

Malware.title: Malware

MalwareClassificationIdsEnum.description: The list of normalized identifiers of the malware classifications.
MalwareClassificationIdsEnum.title: MalwareClassificationIdsEnum

MalwareScanInfo.description:
The malware scan information object describes characteristics, metadata of a
malware scanning job.

end_time.description: The timestamp indicating when the scan job completed execution.

name.description:
The administrator-supplied or application-generated name of the scan. For
example: "Home office weekly user database scan", "Scan folders for viruses",
"Full system virus scan"

num_files.description: The total number of files analyzed during the scan.

num_infected.description: The total number of files identified as infected with malware during the scan.

num_volumes.description: The total number of storage volumes examined during the malware scan.

size.description: The total size in bytes of all files that were scanned.

start_time.description: The timestamp indicating when the scan job began execution.

type.description: The type of scan.

type_id.description: The type id of the scan.

uid.description: The application-defined unique identifier assigned to an instance of a scan.

unique_malware_count.description: The number of unique malware detected across all infected files.

MalwareScanInfo.title: MalwareScanInfo

ManagedEntity.description: The Managed Entity object describes the type and version of an entity, such as a user, device, or policy. For types in the type_id enum list, an associated attribute should be populated. If the type of entity is not in the type_id list, information can be put into the data attribute, type_id should be 'Other' and the type attribute should label the entity type.

data.description: The managed entity content as a JSON object.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

group.description: The group object associated with an entity such as user, policy, or rule.

location.description: The detailed geographical location usually associated with an IP address.

name.description: The name of the managed entity. It should match the name of the specific entity object's name if populated, or the name of the managed entity if the type_id is 'Other'.

org.description: The Organization object containing details about the managed organizational entity. This object includes properties such as the organization name, unique identifier, type, and other organizational metadata. This attribute should be populated when type_id is 4 (Organization).

policy.description: Describes details of a managed policy.

type.description: The managed entity type. For example: Policy, User, Organization, Device.

type_id.description: The type of the Managed Entity. It is recommended to also populate the type attribute with the associated label, or the source specific name if Other.

uid.description: The identifier of the managed entity. It should match the uid of the specific entity's object UID if populated, or the source specific ID if the type_id is 'Other'.

user.description: The user that pertains to the event or object.

version.description: The version of the managed entity. For example: 1.2.3.

ManagedEntity.title: ManagedEntity

ManagedEntityTypeIdEnum.description: The type of the Managed Entity. It is recommended to also populate the type attribute with the associated label, or the source specific name if Other.
ManagedEntityTypeIdEnum.title: ManagedEntityTypeIdEnum

MemoryActivity.description:
Memory Activity events report when a process has memory allocated,
read/modified, or other manipulation activities - such as a buffer overflow or
turning off data execution protection (DEP).

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

actual_permissions.description: The permissions that were granted to access memory.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

base_address.description: The memory address that was access or requested.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

process.description:
The process that had memory allocated, read/written, or had other manipulation
activities performed on it.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

requested_permissions.description: The permissions mask that was requested to access memory.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

size.description: The memory size that was access or requested.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

MemoryActivity.title: MemoryActivity

MessageContext.description:
Communication context for AI system interactions including protocols, roles,
clients, and session information for MCP and other AI communication systems.

ai_role_id.description:
Specifies the functional role of the AI within the context of this message,
such as retrieving information, assisting reasoning, executing a tool, or
generating content.

application.description:
The initiating client application. In AI systems, this represents the
client-side application or framework that initiates requests (e.g., LangChain
application, web browser, mobile app, SDK implementation).

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the model's response/completion for this message.

name.description:
The name or identifier of the message context. In AI systems, this could be the
conversation ID, session name, thread identifier, or interaction name (e.g.,
'user-session-123', 'conversation-abc', 'chat-thread-456').

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the input prompt for this message.

service.description:
The server or service handling the request. In AI systems, this represents the
AI service, API endpoint, or agent that processes and responds to requests
(e.g., OpenAI API service, Claude API service, internal AI model service).

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used for this message (prompt + completion).

uid.description:
The unique identifier of the message context. This could be a session ID,
conversation ID, or other unique identifier that allows correlation of messages
within the same context.

MessageContext.title: MessageContext

MessageContextAiRoleIdEnum.description:
Specifies the functional role of the AI within the context of this message,
such as retrieving information, assisting reasoning, executing a tool, or
generating content.
MessageContextAiRoleIdEnum.title: MessageContextAiRoleIdEnum

Metadata.description: The Metadata object describes the metadata associated with the event.

correlation_uid.description: A unique identifier used to correlate this OCSF event with other related OCSF events, distinct from the event's uid value. This enables linking multiple OCSF events that are part of the same activity, transaction, or security incident across different systems or time periods.

data_classification.description:
The Data Classification object includes information about data classification
levels and data category types.

data_classifications.description:
A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data
classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

debug.description:
Debug information about non-fatal issues with this OCSF event. Each issue is a
line in this string array.

event_code.description:
The identifier of the original event. For example the numerical Windows Event
Code or Cisco syslog code.

extension.description: The schema extension used to create the event.

extensions.description: The schema extensions used to create the event.

is_truncated.description: Indicates whether the OCSF event data has been truncated due to size limitations. When true, some event data may have been omitted to fit within system constraints.

labels.description: The list of labels attached to the event. For example: ["sample", "dev"]

log_format.description:
The format of data in the log where the data originated. For example CSV, XML,
Windows Multiline, JSON, syslog or Cisco Log Schema.

log_level.description:
The level at which an event was logged. This can be log provider specific. For
example the audit level.

log_name.description:
The event log name, typically for the consumer of the event. For example, the
storage bucket name, SIEM repository index name, etc.

log_provider.description:
The logging provider or logging service that logged the event. For example AWS
CloudWatch or Splunk.

log_source.description:
The log system or component where the data originated. For example, a file
path, syslog server name or a Windows hostname and logging subsystem such as
Security.

log_version.description:
The event log schema version of the original event. For example the syslog
version or the Cisco Log Schema version

logged_time.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.

loggers.description:
An array of Logger objects that describe the pipeline of devices and logging
products between the event source and its eventual destination. Note, this
attribute can be used when there is a complex end-to-end path of event flow
and/or to track the chain of custody of the data.

modified_time.description: The time when the event was last modified or enriched.

original_event_uid.description:
The unique identifier assigned to the event in its original logging system
before transformation to OCSF format. This field preserves the source system's
native event identifier, enabling traceability back to the raw log entry. For
example, a Windows Event Record ID, a syslog message ID, a Splunk _cd value, or
a database transaction log sequence number.

original_time.description:
The original event time as reported by the event source. For example, the time
in the original format from system event log such as Syslog on Unix/Linux and
the System event file on Windows. Omit if event is generated instead of
collected via logs.

processed_time.description: The event processed time, such as an ETL operation.

product.description: The product that reported the event.

profiles.description: The list of profiles used to create the event. Profiles should be referenced by their name attribute for core profiles, or extension/name for profiles from extensions.

reporter.description: The entity from which the event or finding was first reported.

sequence.description:
Sequence number of the event. The sequence number is a value available in some
events, to make the exact ordering of events unambiguous, regardless of the
event time precision.

source.description:
The source of the event or finding. This can be any distinguishing name for the
logical origin of the data — for example, 'CloudTrail Events', or a use case
like 'Attack Simulations' or 'Vulnerability Scans'.

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the event.

total_queued_duration.description: The amount of time an event spent in a queue awaiting processing. In this case, the value is the difference between processed_time and logged_time. This duration is inclusive of all queues between the originator of the event and the intended long-term storage destination of the event.

transformation_info_list.description:
An array of transformation info that describes the mappings or transforms
applied to the data.

transmit_time.description:
The time when the event was transmitted from the logging device to it's next
destination.

type.description: The type of the event or finding as a subset of the source of the event. This can be any distinguishing characteristic of the data. For example 'Management Events' or 'Device Penetration Test'.

uid.description: A unique identifier assigned to the OCSF event. This ID is specific to the OCSF event itself and is distinct from the original event identifier in the source system (see original_event_uid).

untruncated_size.description: The original size of the OCSF event data in kilobytes before any truncation occurred. This field is typically populated when is_truncated is true to indicate the full size of the original event.

version.description: The version of the OCSF schema, using Semantic Versioning Specification (SemVer). For example: 1.0.0. Event consumers use the version to determine the available event attributes.

Metadata.title: Metadata

Metric.description: The Metric object defines a simple name/value pair entity for a metric.

name.description: The name of the metric.

Metric.title: Metric

Mitigation.description:
The MITRE Mitigation object describes the ATT&CK® or ATLAS™ Mitigation ID
and/or name that is associated to an attack.

countermeasures.description: The D3FEND countermeasures that are associated with the attack technique. For example: ATT&CK Technique T1003 is addressed by Mitigation M1027, and D3FEND Technique D3-OTP.

name.description: The Mitigation name that is associated with the attack technique. For example: Password Policies, or Code Signing.

src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the Mitigation. For example: https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v14/mitigations/M1027.

uid.description: The Mitigation ID that is associated with the attack technique. For example: M1027, or AML.M0013.

Mitigation.title: Mitigation

Module.description: The Module object describes the attributes of a module.

base_address.description: The memory address where the module was loaded.

file.description: The module file object.

function_invocation.description: Details about the invocation of the function given in function_name.

function_name.description:
The invoked function in the module. For load and unload events, this is the
entry-point function of the module. The system calls the entry-point function
whenever a process or thread loads or unloads the module.

load_type.description:
The load type, normalized to the caption of the load_type_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

load_type_id.description: The normalized identifier for how the module was loaded in memory.

start_address.description: The start address of the execution.

type.description: The module type.

Module.title: Module

ModuleActivity.description: Module Activity events report when an endpoint process acts on a module.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description: The actor that performed the activity on the target module. For example, the process that loaded a module into memory.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

module.description: The module that was loaded, unloaded, or invoked.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an

registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

ModuleActivity.title: ModuleActivity

ModuleLoadTypeIdEnum.title: ModuleLoadTypeIdEnum

ModuleQuery.description: Module Query events report information about loaded modules.

system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

module.description: The module that pertains to the event.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

process.description: The process that loaded the module.

query_info.description: The search details associated with the query request.

query_result_id.description: The normalized identifier of the query result.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

ModuleQuery.title: ModuleQuery

NetworkActivity.description: Network Activity events report network connection and traffic activity.

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

actor.description:
The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the
source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign
but may be part of a campaign.

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

app_name.description:
The network application name identified by tools such as NBAR or App ID (e.g.,
youtube, facebook, webex). This represents a specific network application that
uses standard protocols (such as https or quic) to deliver its service.

app_protocol_name.description:
The application-layer (Layer 7) protocol name identified by deep packet inspection or packet parsing (e.g., https, quic, ssh, dns), expressed as an IANA-registered service name from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number Registry.

Note: Port numbers alone are not always a reliable indicator of the actual application protocol in use.

attacks.description:
An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques &
sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics,
techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.description:
Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any
associated policies related to the activity/event.

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value.

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value.

class_uid.description:
The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in
an event.

cloud.description:
Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was
created.

confidence.description:
The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the
case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.description:
The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the
finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and
may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

cumulative_traffic.description: The cumulative (running total) network traffic aggregated from the start of a flow or session. Use when reporting: (1) total accumulated bytes/packets since flow initiation, (2) combined aggregation models where both incremental deltas and running totals are reported together (populate both traffic for the delta and this attribute for the cumulative total), or (3) final summary metrics when a long-lived connection closes. This represents the sum of all activity from flow start to the current observation, not a delta or point-in-time value.

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

disposition.description:
The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In
the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.description:
Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access
control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

dst_endpoint.description: The destination endpoint of the network activity — typically the responder. In bi-flow or asymmetric flow scenarios, this endpoint may represent the true initiator of the original communication. Use initiator_id to determine which endpoint actually initiated the communication.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description:
The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in
the aggregate event.

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

firewall_rule.description:
The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable.

initiator.description: The endpoint that initiated the network communication, normalized to the caption of initiator_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

initiator_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the endpoint that initiated the network
communication. Use this field to disambiguate source and destination roles in
bi-directional or asymmetric flow scenarios.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

is_src_dst_assignment_known.description: true denotes that src_endpoint and dst_endpoint correctly identify the initiator and responder respectively. false denotes that the event source has arbitrarily assigned one peer to src_endpoint and the other to dst_endpoint, in other words that initiator and responder are not being asserted. This can occur, for example, when the event source is a network appliance that has not observed the initiation of a given connection. In the absence of this attribute, interpretation of the initiator and responder is implementation-specific.

ja4_fingerprint_list.description: A list of the JA4+ network fingerprints.

load_balancer.description:
The Load Balancer object contains information related to the device that is
distributing incoming traffic to specified destinations.

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.description:
Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target
system.

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

network_observation_point.description: The network endpoint that observes or inspects network traffic as a third-party system, used when the observer is neither the source nor the destination of the communication (when observation_point_id = 3). Examples include network taps, span ports, inline security devices, or packet capture systems that monitor traffic between other endpoints.

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

observation_point.description: Indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity. The value is normalized to the caption of the observation_point_id.

observation_point_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the observation point. The observation point
identifier indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network
endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity.

osint.description:
The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an
indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation,
registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual
information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or
finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

packet_list.description: The list of packet objects describing captured network packets.

policy.description:
The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if
applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control
policy.

proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_connection_info.description: The connection information from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_endpoint.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_http_request.description: The HTTP Request from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_http_response.description: The HTTP Response from the remote server to the proxy server.

proxy_tls.description: The TLS protocol negotiated between the proxy server and the remote server.

proxy_traffic.description:
The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from
proxy to remote server at a given point of time.

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

src_endpoint.description: The source endpoint of the network activity — typically the initiator. In bi-flow or asymmetric flow scenarios, this endpoint may represent the response side of communication originally initiated by the destination. Use initiator_id to determine which endpoint actually initiated the communication.

start_time.description:
The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included
in the aggregate event.

status.description:
The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case
of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.description:
The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding
outcome.

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

unmapped.description:
The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of
those attributes are specific to the event source.

url.description: The URL details relevant to the network traffic.

NetworkActivity.title: NetworkActivity

NetworkActivityInitiatorIdEnum.description:
The normalized identifier of the endpoint that initiated the network
communication. Use this field to disambiguate source and destination roles in
bi-directional or asymmetric flow scenarios.
NetworkActivityInitiatorIdEnum.title: NetworkActivityInitiatorIdEnum

NetworkConnectionInfo.description:
The Network Connection Information object describes characteristics of an OSI
Transport Layer communication, including TCP and UDP.

boundary.description:
The boundary of the connection, normalized to the caption of 'boundary_id'. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

For cloud connections, this translates to the traffic-boundary(same VPC, through IGW, etc.). For traditional networks, this is described as Local, Internal, or External.

boundary_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the boundary of the connection.

For cloud connections, this translates to the traffic-boundary (same VPC, through IGW, etc.). For traditional networks, this is described as Local, Internal, or External.

community_uid.description: The Community ID of the network connection.

direction.description:
The direction of the initiated connection, traffic, or email, normalized to the
caption of the direction_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the
event source.

direction_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the direction of the initiated connection,
traffic, or email.

flag_history.description: The Connection Flag History summarizes events in a network connection. For example flags ShAD representing SYN, SYN/ACK, ACK and Data exchange.

protocol_name.description: The IP protocol name in lowercase, as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA). For example: tcp or udp.

protocol_num.description: The IP protocol number, as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA). For example: 6 for TCP and 17 for UDP.

session.description: The authenticated user or service session.

tcp_flags.description: The network connection TCP header flags (i.e., control bits).

uid.description: The unique identifier of the connection.

NetworkConnectionInfo.title: NetworkConnectionInfo

NetworkConnectionQuery.description:
Network Connection Query events report information about active network
connections.

NetworkConnectionQuery.title: NetworkConnectionQuery

NetworkEndpoint.description:
The Network Endpoint object describes characteristics of a network endpoint.
These can be a source or destination of a network connection.

agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

autonomous_system.description: The Autonomous System details associated with an IP address.

container.description:
The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a
prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system
using a container runtime like containerd.

domain.description:
The name of the domain that the endpoint belongs to or that corresponds to the
endpoint.

fingerprints.description:
Fingerprints that identify the specific application implementation on this
endpoint, such as Cisco NPF or HASSH.

hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the endpoint.

instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.

interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).

interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.

intermediate_ips.description:
The intermediate IP Addresses. For example, the IP addresses in the HTTP
X-Forwarded-For header.

ip.description: The IP address of the endpoint, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

isp.description: The name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP).

isp_org.description:
The organization name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP). This represents
the parent organization or company that owns/operates the ISP. For example,
Comcast Corporation would be the ISP org for Xfinity internet service. This
attribute helps identify the ultimate provider when ISPs operate under
different brand names.

location.description: The geographical location of the endpoint.

mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.

mac_vendor.description:
The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller
(NIC), as identified from the MAC address.

name.description: The short name of the endpoint.

namespace_pid.description:
If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process
identifier within that process namespace.

network_scope.description: Indicates whether the endpoint resides inside the customer’s network, outside on the Internet, or if its location relative to the customer’s network cannot be determined. The value is normalized to the caption of the network_scope_id.

network_scope_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the endpoint’s network scope. The normalized
network scope identifier indicates whether the endpoint resides inside the
customer’s network, outside on the Internet, or if its location relative to the
customer’s network cannot be determined.

owner.description:
The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last
logged into it.

pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.

port.description: The port used for communication within the network connection.

proxy_endpoint.description:
The network proxy information pertaining to a specific endpoint. This can be
used to describe information pertaining to network address translation (NAT).

subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.

svc_name.description:
The service name in service-to-service connections. For example, AWS VPC logs
the pkt-src-aws-service and pkt-dst-aws-service fields identify the connection
is coming from or going to an AWS service.

type.description: The network endpoint type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.

type_id.description: The network endpoint type ID.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint.

vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.

NetworkEndpoint.title: NetworkEndpoint

NetworkEvent.description:
Network event is a generic event that defines a set of attributes available in
the Network category.
